functions.name: AppleScript.Close
functions.description: Removes a Script from memory. Not really necessary since scripts don't take much space.

parameters.name: Script ID
parameters.description: The ID returned by the " AppleScript.Compile " function.

examples.label: Remove A compiled Script from memory
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.Close" ; $scriptID )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AppleScript.Compile
functions.description: Compiles the script and returns the Script ID. You can use the Script ID to run the script using the AppleScript.Execute function. The compiled script is stored in memory. This is useful if you have large script that needs to be run over and over again.

parameters.name: Script Text
parameters.description: This is the text of the script to compile

examples.label: Compile a script
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.Compile" ; $Applescript )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Send email via AppleScript and properties:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$script
; Value: "property MySubject : \"My Subject\"
property MyBody : \"My Body\"
property MyReceiver : \"test@test.test\"
property MySender : \"test@test.test\"
tell application \"Mail\"
activate
set NewMail to make new outgoing message with properties {visible:true, subject:MySubject, content:MyBody}
tell NewMail
make new to recipient at beginning of to recipients with properties {address:MyReceiver}
end tell
set the sender of NewMail to MySender
end tell" ]
# compile it
Set Variable [
$scriptID
; Value:
MBS
( "Applescript.Compile";
$script
]
If [
MBS
("isError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [
MBS
("
AppleScript.LastErrorMessage
") ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
# now fill in values
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.SetPropertyValue
";
$scriptID
; "MySubject";
AppleScript Properties::Subject
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.SetPropertyValue
";
$scriptID
; "MyBody";
AppleScript Properties::Body
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.SetPropertyValue
";
$scriptID
; "MyReceiver";
AppleScript Properties::Receiver
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.SetPropertyValue
";
$scriptID
; "MySender";
AppleScript Properties::Sender
) ]
# check if it is there
Show Custom Dialog [
MBS
("
AppleScript.GetPropertyValue
";
$scriptID
; "MySubject") ]
# run the script
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.Execute
";
$scriptID
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.Close
";
$scriptID
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"property MySubject : \"My Subject\"property MyBody : \"My Body\"property MyReceiver : \"test@test.test\"property MySender : \"test@test.test\"tell application \"Mail\"    activate    set NewMail to make new outgoing message with properties {visible:true, subject:MySubject, content:MyBody}        tell NewMail        make new to recipient at beginning of to recipients with properties {address:MyReceiver}    end tell        set the sender of NewMail to MySenderend tell" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$script</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> compile it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.Compile"; $script </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$scriptID</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("isError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> MBS("AppleScript.LastErrorMessage") </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now fill in values</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.SetPropertyValue"; $scriptID; "MySubject"; AppleScript Properties::Subject) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.SetPropertyValue"; $scriptID; "MyBody"; AppleScript Properties::Body) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.SetPropertyValue"; $scriptID; "MyReceiver"; AppleScript Properties::Receiver) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.SetPropertyValue"; $scriptID; "MySender"; AppleScript Properties::Sender) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> check if it is there</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> MBS("Applescript.GetPropertyValue"; $scriptID</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"MySubject") </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> run the script</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.Execute"; $scriptID) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.Close"; $scriptID) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AppleScript.DeterminePermissionToAutomateTarget
functions.description: Checks whether AppleScript is allowed. Determines whether the current application is able to send an AppleEvent with the given eventClass and eventID to the application described as target via bundle ID. Mac OS 10.14 and later impose additional requirements on applications when they send AppleEvents to other applications in order to insure that users are aware of and consent to allowing such control or information exchange.  Generally this involves the user being prompted in a secure fashion the first time an application attempts to send an AppleEvent to another application. If the user consents then this application can send events to the target.  If the user does not consent then any future attempts to send AppleEvents will result in a failure with errAEEventNotPermitted being returned. Certain AppleEvents are allowed to be sent without prompting the user. Pass typeWildCard (****) for the eventClass and eventID to determine if every event is allowed to be sent from this application to the target. Applications can determine, without sending an AppleEvent to a target application, whether they are allowed to send AppleEvents to the target with this function.  If askUserIfNeeded is true, and this application does not yet have permission to send AppleEvents to the target, then the user will be asked if permission can be granted; if askUserIfNeeded is 0 and permission has not been granted, then WouldRequireUserConsent will be returned. The target bundle ID must refer to an already running application. If the current application is permitted to send the given AppleEvent to the target, then OK will be returned.  If the current application is not permitted to send the event, NotPermitted will be returned.  If the target application is not running, then ApplicationNotRunning will be returned.  If askUserIfNeeded is false, and this application is not yet permitted to send AppleEvents to the target, then WouldRequireUserConsent will be returned. Requires MacOS 10.14 or newer. May not work in FileMaker 18 due to missing entitlements for the FileMaker app. You can try FileMaker 17 instead, where it works. Or run FileMaker 18 from Terminal, where it also works.

parameters.name: BundleID
parameters.description: The bundle ID of the application to control. The target address descriptor must refer to a running application.  If the target application is on another machine, then Remote AppleEvents must be enabled on that machine for the user.

parameters.name: AEEventClass
parameters.description: The event class of the Apple event to determine permission for. **** for wildcard to use all.

parameters.name: AEEventID
parameters.description: The event ID of the Apple event to determine permission for. **** for wildcard to use all.

parameters.name: askUserIfNeeded
parameters.description: Whether dialog can show to ask user if needed. Pass 1 to allow dialog or 0 to disallow. If 1, and if this application does not yet have permission to send events to the target application, then prompt the user to obtain permission.  If 0, do not prompt the user. Default is 0.

functions.result: Returns OK, ApplicationNotRunning, NotPermitted, WouldRequireUserConsent, or error.

examples.label: Ask for permissions for Safari:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AppleScript.Execute
functions.description: Performs a compiled Apple Script. Executes an AppleScript that was previously compiled using the AppleScript.Compile function. AppleScripts can return a result to the object that called them. This is similar to the Script Results that were introduced in FileMaker 8.0. Older plugin versions used to put quotes around result. 6.4 and newer don't include quotes.

parameters.name: Script ID
parameters.description: The Id of the script you want to execute. This ID returned by the AppleScript.Compile function.

examples.label: Execute a stored Applescript
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.Execute" ; $ScriptID)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AppleScript.GetPropertyName
functions.description: Queries name of property with given index. Last error is set.

parameters.name: Script ID
parameters.description: The Id of the script you want to execute. This ID returned by the AppleScript.Compile function.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the property from 0 to AppleScript.PropertyCount -1.

examples.label: List properties by name:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$scriptID
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.Compile
";
AppleScript Properties::AppleScriptCode
) ]
If [
MBS
("isError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ Message:
MBS
("
AppleScript.LastErrorMessage
"); Buttons: “OK”, “Cancel” ]
Exit Script [ ]
End If
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.PropertyCount
";
$scriptID
) ]
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:"" ]
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:0 ]
If [ GetAsNumber(
$count
) > 0 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$n
; Value:
MBS
( "Applescript.GetPropertyName";
$ScriptID
;
$i
) ]
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
$list
&
$n
& ¶ ]
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:
$i
+1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$count
=
$i
]
End Loop
Show Custom Dialog [
$count
& " Properties";
$list
]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
AppleScript.Close
";
$scriptID
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.Compile"; AppleScript Properties::AppleScriptCode ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$scriptID</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("isError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> Message: MBS("AppleScript.LastErrorMessage")</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>Buttons: “OK”, “Cancel” </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.PropertyCount"; $scriptID) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> GetAsNumber($count) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.GetPropertyName"; $ScriptID; $i ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$n</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$list & $n & ¶ </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$i+1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $count = $i </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> $count & " Properties"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$list </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Applescript.Close"; $scriptID) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AppleScript.LastErrorPosition

functions.name: AppleScript.Run
functions.description: The function compiles the AppleScript text and runs it. Any Result returned by the AppleScript is returned as the result of the calculation. This is similar to the Script Results that were introduced in FileMaker 8.0. This is much easier then using AppleScript.Compile , AppleScript.Execute and AppleScript.Close Older plugin versions used to put quotes around result. 6.4 and newer don't include quotes. This function does return "" in case AppleScript fails. If you need more error handling, please run AppleScript.Compile to get compile error for syntax errors.

parameters.name: Script Text
parameters.description: This is the text of the script to run

examples.label: Trigger a FileMaker Script Using OS Scripting
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
// --- the name of the script to run ------------------

ScriptName = "Triggered Script";
FileName = Get(FileName);

//------------------------------------------------------------

//--- don't need to edit anything below this line --------


Applescript = "do script " & Quote(ScriptName);

VBScriptText = 
"FUNCTION DoFMSCript(FileName, ScriptName)" & "¶" & 

"Set fmApp = CreateObject(\"FMPro.Application\")" & "¶" & 
"fmApp.Visible = True" & "¶" & 
"Set fmDocs = fmApp.Documents" & "¶" &
"For Each fmDoc In fmDocs" & "¶" &
"If InStr(LCase(fmDoc.fullname), LCase(FileName)) > 0 Then" & "¶" &
" fmDoc.dofmscript (ScriptName)" & "¶" &
"End If" & "¶" &
"Next" & "¶" &

"END FUNCTION";

ScriptID = MBS("WindowsScript.Create");
lang= MBS("WindowsScript.SetLanguage"; ScriptID; "VBScript");
addcode = MBS("WindowsScript.AddCode"; ScriptID; VBScriptText)

];
Case(
Get ( SystemPlatform ) = 1;

// Mac OSX Case
MBS( 
"Applescript.Run";
Applescript
);

// Windows Case
MBS("WindowsScript.ExecuteFunction";ScriptID; "DoFMScript";FileName; ScriptName) & 
MBS("WindowsScript.Close"; ScriptID)

)
)
</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create A Folder
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
/*-----------------PARAMETERS--------------------*/
folderName = "My New Folder";

scriptText = 
"set folder_name to " & Quote(folderName) & "¶" &
"tell application " & Quote("Finder") & "¶" & 
"	set f to make new folder at desktop" & "¶" & 
"	set the name of f to folder_name" & "¶" & 
"end tell"

];
/*-------------------FUNCTION----------------------*/
MBS( 
"Applescript.Run";
scriptText
)
)

/*------------------------------------------/
Creates a folder called "My New Folder on the users desktop
*/</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Trigger A FileMaker Script (Custom Function)
examples.text_code:
MBS_TriggerScript ( "Triggered Script" ; Get ( FileName ) )
Custom Function Definition
/*###############################################
MBS_TriggerScript
created 10/26/06, by Todd Geist, todd@geistinteractive.com
Parameters: theScriptName, theFileName
Dependancies:
MBS
FileMaker Plug-in.
Notes: Uses VBScript and Applescript to run a script
################################################*/
Let(
[
Applescript = "do script " & Quote(theScriptName);
VBScriptText =
"FUNCTION DoFMSCript(FileName, ScriptName)" & "¶" &
"Set fmApp = CreateObject(\"FMPro.Application\")" & "¶" &
"fmApp.Visible = True" & "¶" &
"Set fmDocs = fmApp.Documents" & "¶" &
"For Each fmDoc In fmDocs" & "¶" &
"If InStr(LCase(fmDoc.fullname), LCase(FileName)) > 0 Then" & "¶" &
" fmDoc.dofmscript (ScriptName)" & "¶" &
"End If" & "¶" &
"Next" & "¶" &
"END FUNCTION";
ScriptID =
MBS
("
WindowsScript.Create
");
lang=
MBS
("
WindowsScript.SetLanguage
"; ScriptID; "VBScript");
addcode =
MBS
("
WindowsScript.AddCode
"; ScriptID; VBScriptText)
];
Case(
Get ( SystemPlatform ) = 1;
// Mac OSX Case
MBS
(
"Applescript.Run";
Applescript
);
// Windows Case
MBS
("
WindowsScript.ExecuteFunction
";ScriptID; "DoFMScript";theFileName; theScriptName) &
MBS
("
WindowsScript.Close
"; ScriptID)
)
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS_TriggerScript ( "Triggered Script" ; Get ( FileName ) ) 


Custom Function Definition
/*###############################################

MBS_TriggerScript
created 10/26/06, by Todd Geist, todd@geistinteractive.com

Parameters: theScriptName, theFileName

Dependancies: MBS FileMaker Plug-in.

Notes: Uses VBScript and Applescript to run a script

################################################*/
Let(
[

Applescript = "do script " & Quote(theScriptName);

VBScriptText = 
"FUNCTION DoFMSCript(FileName, ScriptName)" & "¶" & 

"Set fmApp = CreateObject(\"FMPro.Application\")" & "¶" & 
"fmApp.Visible = True" & "¶" & 
"Set fmDocs = fmApp.Documents" & "¶" &
"For Each fmDoc In fmDocs" & "¶" &
"If InStr(LCase(fmDoc.fullname), LCase(FileName)) > 0 Then" & "¶" &
" fmDoc.dofmscript (ScriptName)" & "¶" &
"End If" & "¶" &
"Next" & "¶" &

"END FUNCTION";

ScriptID = MBS("WindowsScript.Create");
lang= MBS("WindowsScript.SetLanguage"; ScriptID; "VBScript");
addcode = MBS("WindowsScript.AddCode"; ScriptID; VBScriptText)

];
Case(
Get ( SystemPlatform ) = 1;

// Mac OSX Case
MBS( 
"Applescript.Run";
Applescript
);

// Windows Case
MBS("WindowsScript.ExecuteFunction";ScriptID; "DoFMScript";theFileName; theScriptName) & 
MBS("WindowsScript.Close"; ScriptID)

)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Speak using AppleScript:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$text
; Value: "Hello \"Test\"" ]
Set Variable [
$voice
; Value: "Daniel" ]
Set Variable [
$rate
; Value: 100 ]
Set Variable [
$pitch
; Value: 60 ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value: Substitute(
$text
; "\""; "\\\"") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "AppleScript.Run"; "say \"" &
$text
& "\" using \"" &
$voice
& "\" speaking rate " &
$rate
& " pitch " &
$pitch
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Hello \"Test\"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Daniel" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$voice</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>100 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$rate</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>60 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pitch</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Substitute($text; "\""; "\\\"") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "AppleScript.Run"; "say \"" & $text & "\" using \"" & $voice & "\" speaking rate " & $rate & " pitch " & $pitch ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: AppleScript.SetPropertyValue
functions.description: Sets property value with new text. Lasterror is set.

parameters.name: Script ID
parameters.description: The Id of the script you want to execute. This ID returned by the AppleScript.Compile function.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the property.

parameters.name: Value

functions.name: CFunction.AllocateArray
functions.description: Allocates an array parameter. See table below for valid types.

parameters.name: FunctionRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the function.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index for the parameter. Range is 0 to CFunction.ParameterCount -1.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The data type to use.

parameters.name: Count
parameters.description: The number of elements to allocate.

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CFunction.Call
functions.description: Calls a C function. Pass a FileMaker parameter for each parameter. For pointer parameter, you can use Function.AllocateArray to pass an array as parameter. The passed value is then ignored and we pass pointer to array. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Parameters parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: FunctionRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the function.

parameters.name: Parameters...
parameters.description: The parameters. Pass one for each value, see examples.

examples.label: Call a function without parameters:
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Load SQLite version function and run it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$library
; Value:
MBS
( "
CLibrary.Load
"; "/usr/lib/libsqlite3.dylib" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load library." ;
$library
]
Else
Set Variable [
$function
; Value:
MBS
( "
CLibrary.LoadFunction
";
$library
; "sqlite3_libversion"; "()Z" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load function." ;
$function
]
Else
Set Variable [
$version
; Value:
MBS
( "CFunction.Call";
$function
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "SQLite version:" ;
$version
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CFunction.Release
";
$function
) ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CLibrary.Release
";
$library
) ]
End If

examples.label: Query user name on Windows:
examples.text_code:
# this is C declaration:
# BOOL GetUserNameW(LPWSTR lpBuffer, LPDWORD pcbBuffer);
#
# we load the library and the function
Set Variable [
$library
; Value:
MBS
( "
CLibrary.Load
"; "Advapi32.dll") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load library." ;
$library
]
Else
Set Variable [
$function
; Value:
MBS
( "
CLibrary.LoadFunction
";
$library
; "GetUserNameW"; "(pp)i") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to load library." ;
$function
]
Else
# since 1st parameter is WChar Array, we allocate space for it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CFunction.AllocateArray
";
$function
; 0; "S"; 256 ) ]
# and for the count variable as ptr, we allocate a one value array
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CFunction.AllocateArray
";
$function
; 1; "I"; 1) ]
# put in 255 for the length of the first array (minus one character for end character)
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CFunction.SetArray
";
$function
; 1; "i"; 255) ]
# Now call function
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "CFunction.Call";
$function
) ]
If [
$result
= 1 ]
# now we read how many characters we got
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CFunction.GetArray
";
$function
; 1; "I") ]
Set Variable [
$length
; Value: GetValue(
$r
; 1) ]
# and get the characters
Set Variable [
$characters
; Value:
MBS
( "
CFunction.GetArray
";
$function
; 0; "S") ]
#
# we loop over the values to build the text
Set Variable [
$text
; Value: "" ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 1 ]
Loop [ Flush: Always ]
Set Variable [
$char
; Value: GetValue(
$characters
;
$index
) ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
$text
& Char(
$char
) ]
#
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
=
$length
]
End Loop
#
Show Custom Dialog [ "Windows user name" ;
$text
]
#
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CFunction.FreeArray
";
$function
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CFunction.FreeArray
";
$function
; 1) ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CFunction.Release
";
$function
) ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
CLibrary.Release
";
$library
) ]
End If

    <Text> put in 255 for the length of the first array (minus one character for end character)</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CFunction.SetArray"; $function; 1; "i"; 255) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Now call function</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CFunction.Call"; $function) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $result = 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now we read how many characters we got</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CFunction.GetArray"; $function; 1; "I") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($r; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$length</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and get the characters</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CFunction.GetArray"; $function; 0; "S") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$characters</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> we loop over the values to build the text</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($characters; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$char</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$text & Char($char) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index = $length </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Windows user name" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$text </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CFunction.FreeArray"; $function; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CFunction.FreeArray"; $function; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CFunction.Release"; $function) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CLibrary.Release"; $library) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CLGeocoder.Cancel
functions.description: Cancels a pending geocoding request. You can use this method to cancel a pending request and free up the resources associated with that request. Canceling a pending request causes the completion handler block to be called. If the request is not pending, because it has already returned or has not yet begun, this method does nothing.

parameters.name: Geocoder
parameters.description: The reference number of the geocoder instance.

functions.name: CLGeocoder.Cancelled
functions.description: Whether the request was cancelled.

parameters.name: Geocoder
parameters.description: The reference to the geocoder

functions.name: CLGeocoder.Close
functions.description: Closes a request and frees memory. Call this to close the request.

parameters.name: Geocoder
parameters.description: The reference number of the geocoder instance.

functions.name: CLGeocoder.Distance
functions.description: Returns the distance (in meters) of the two locations. This method measures the distance between the two locations by tracing a line between them that follows the curvature of the Earth. The resulting arc is a smooth curve and does not take into account specific altitude changes between the two locations.

parameters.name: latitude1
parameters.description: The latitude value for the geo coordinate.

parameters.name: longitude1
parameters.description: The latitude value for the geo coordinate.

parameters.name: latitude2
parameters.description: The latitude value for the geo coordinate.

parameters.name: longitude2
parameters.description: The latitude value for the geo coordinate.

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "CLGeocoder.Distance"; 51.50939; -0.11832; 52.518611; 13.408333)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: CLGeocoder.GeocodeAddressString
functions.description: Submits a forward-geocoding request using the specified string. This method submits the specified location data to the geocoding server asynchronously and returns. Your completion handler block will be executed on the main thread. After initiating a forward-geocoding request, do not attempt to initiate another forward- or reverse-geocoding request. Geocoding requests are rate-limited for each app, so making too many requests in a short period of time may cause some of the requests to fail. When the maximum rate is exceeded, the geo coder passes an error back. You need to use CLGeocoder.Close later to free the geo coder. Seems to not work on server as it always times out.

parameters.name: Address
parameters.description: A string describing the location you want to look up. For example, you could specify the string "1 Infinite Loop, Cupertino, CA"” to locate Apple headquarters.

parameters.name: Wait
parameters.description: Pass 1 to wait for geocoder to have a result. Pass 0 to not wait and check later if geocoder is done.

examples.label: Geocodes an address:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CLGeocoder.GeocodeAddressString"; CLGeocoder::AddressString)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query in one let statement and return JSON or error:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
	Address = "Berlin, Germany";
	// run query and wait a bit
	g = MBS( "CLGeocoder.GeocodeAddressString"; Address; 1 );
	e = MBS("IsError");
	// query JSON if we got something
	r = If (e = 0; MBS( "CLGeocoder.JSON"; g ); g);
	// free resources
	f = If (e = 0; MBS( "CLGeocoder.Close"; g ); 0)
]; r )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

	e = MBS("IsError");
	// query JSON if we got something

]; r )</Calculation>

]; r )</Calculation>
    </Value>

functions.name: CLGeocoder.ReverseGeocodeLocation
functions.description: Submits a reverse-geocoding request for the specified location. This method submits the specified location data to the geocoding server asynchronously and returns. Your completion handler block will be executed on the main thread. After initiating a reverse-geocoding request, do not attempt to initiate another reverse- or forward-geocoding request. Geocoding requests are rate-limited for each app, so making too many requests in a short period of time may cause some of the requests to fail. When the maximum rate is exceeded, the geo coder passes an error back. You need to use CLGeocoder.Close later to free the geo coder. CoreLocation geo coder reference numbers are starting at 24000 and counting up for each new geo coder. Seems to not work on server as it always times out.

parameters.name: Latitude
parameters.description: The latitude of the coordinate.

parameters.name: Longitude
parameters.description: The longitude of the coordinate.

parameters.name: Wait
parameters.description: Pass 1 to wait for geocoder to have a result. Pass 0 to not wait and check later if geocoder is done.

examples.label: Reverse geocode location:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("CLGeocoder.ReverseGeocodeLocation"; CLGeocoder::Latitude; CLGeocoder::Longitude)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Run query and get back JSON or error:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
	latitude = 52.517631899999998;
	longitude = 13.4096574;
	// run query and wait a bit
	g = MBS( "CLGeocoder.ReverseGeocodeLocation"; latitude; longitude; 1 );
	e = MBS("IsError");
	// query JSON if we got something
	r = If (e = 0; MBS( "CLGeocoder.JSON"; g ); g);
	// free resources
	f = If (e = 0; MBS( "CLGeocoder.Close"; g ); 0)
]; r )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IKScannerDevicePanel.ImageCount
functions.description: Queries number of images in queue. Either you use the trigger and process each image there or you can call later IKScannerDevicePanel.ImageCount and IKScannerDevicePanel.NextImage to get the images.

functions.name: IKScannerDevicePanel.NextImage
functions.description: Queries path of next image. You can use Container.ReadFile to read file and store it to a container. Either you use the trigger and process each image there or you can call later IKScannerDevicePanel.ImageCount and IKScannerDevicePanel.NextImage to get the images. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

functions.name: IKScannerDevicePanel.SetTrigger
functions.description: Sets the trigger to notify when new picture arrives. You get a call for each picture as user may import several. The parameter to your script is the native file path to the image file. If scan is to PDF and we use document feeder, be aware that you may get a trigger with same file path again if a new page is added. Either you use the trigger and process each image there or you can call later IKScannerDevicePanel.ImageCount and IKScannerDevicePanel.NextImage to get the images. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run.

$r
; Value:
MBS
( "IKScannerDevicePanel.SetTrigger"; Get(FileName); "Scanner Dialog Trigger" )]
examples.xml_code:

    <Value>

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

examples.label: Import image in triggered script:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Path
; Value:Get(ScriptParameter)]
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
("
Container.ReadFile
";
$path
)]
If [
MBS
("ISError") = 0]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
ImageCapture Dialogs::Image
;
$Image
]
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.ReadFile"; $path)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("ISError") = 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$Image</Calculation>
    <Field table="ImageCapture Dialogs" id="99999" name="Image"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IsClient
functions.description: Checks if plugin is running on a client. Returns 0 if running on a server. See also IsRegistered , IsRuntime and IsServer .

examples.label: Run on client:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "IsClient" )]
# runs on client
Else
# runs on server or where no plugin is installed
End If

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [MBS( "IsClient" )]
	# runs on client
Else
	# runs on server or where no plugin is installed
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Platform
functions.description: Returns what platform the plugin is running on. Value can be FileMaker Pro Advanced, FileMaker Pro, Runtime, Server, Web Direct, Mobile, XDBC, ServerScripting or InstantWebPublishing. Older versions may return "Developer" or "Pro" for FIleMaker Pro. For FileMaker 17 or newer, MBS Plugin detects the status of whether Advanced Tools are enabled. Returns Developer if enabled and Pro if not enabled.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Platform" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.text_code:
# Script to trigger somewhere to report back details about
MBS
Plugin
Set Variable [
$version
; Value: GetAsText(
MBS
("Version")) ]
Set Variable [
$platform
; Value:
MBS
( "Platform" ) ]
Set Variable [
$buildNumber
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.BuildNumber
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.Path
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$IsRegistered
; Value:
MBS
( "IsRegistered" ) ]
Set Variable [
$Name
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.LicenseeName
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$Debug
; Value:
MBS
( "
Plugin.isDebug
" ) ]
If [
$version
= "?" ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value: "
MBS
Plugin not installed. " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) ]
Else
Set Variable [
$l1
; Value: "
MBS
Plugin " &
$version
& ", build " &
$buildNumber
& " installed in " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) ]
Set Variable [
$l2
; Value: "¶Platform: " &
$Platform
& "¶Path: " &
$path
]
Set Variable [
$l3
; Value: If(
$
IsRegistered
; "¶Licensed for: " &
$Name
; "¶No license set.") ]
Set Variable [
$l4
; Value: "¶Debug: " & If(
$debug
; "yes"; "no") ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
$l1
&
$l2
&
$l3
]
End If
Exit Script [ Text Result:
$text
]

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Script to trigger somewhere to report back details about MBS Plugin</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsText(MBS("Version")) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$version</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Platform" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$platform</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.BuildNumber" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$buildNumber</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.Path" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "IsRegistered" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$IsRegistered</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.LicenseeName" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Plugin.isDebug" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Debug</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $version = "?" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"MBS Plugin not installed. " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"MBS Plugin " & $version & ", build " & $buildNumber & " installed in " & Get ( ApplicationVersion ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"¶Platform: " & $Platform & "¶Path: " & $path </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If($IsRegistered; "¶Licensed for: " & $Name ; "¶No license set.") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l3</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"¶Debug: " & If($debug; "yes"; "no") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$l4</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$l1 & $l2 & $l3 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: $text </Calculation>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

MBS
( "Process.IsFrontMost" ) = 0 ]
Set Variable [

MBS
( "
Process.SetFrontMost

End If</Calculation>

functions.name: Process.LoadLibrary
functions.description: Loads a given native library. This can be used to resolve dependencies with libraries in various places. This should work with .dll files on Windows, .dylib files on macOS and .so files on Linux. Windows error 193 means that 64-bit FileMaker tried to load 32-bit library or 32-bit FileMaker tried to load 64-bit library. The bit level must match between app and library. For macOS the error message will tell you about wrong architecture. If you like to interface with functions in the DLL, please use CFunction functions. If you have trouble with DLLs not loading on Windows, please check if you have new Visual Studio Runtime libraries installed. If you need more than one DLL, please use Process.SetDllDirectory to point to the folder to find the DLL files. On Linux if you get the error wrong ELF class: ELFCLASS32 if you try to load a 32-bit library into a 64-bit application. You may also get errors about version GLIBC_2.25 or similar if your libc library on the Linux installation isn't new enough for the loaded library. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native path to the library.

examples.label: Load a library:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Process.LoadLibrary"; "C:\Users\Christian\Desktop\dynapdf.dll") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

functions.name: Process.SetDllDirectory
functions.description: Adds a directory to the search path used to locate DLLs for the application. The directory to be added to the search path. If this parameter is an empty string, the call restores the default search order. The SetDllDirectory function affects all subsequent calls to the Process.LoadLibrary functions. It also effectively disables safe DLL search mode while the specified directory is in the search path. After calling SetDllDirectory, the standard DLL search path is: 1. The directory from which the application loaded. 2. The directory specified by the pathName parameter. 3. The system directory. Use the GetSystemDirectory function to get the path of this directory. The name of this directory is System32. 4. The 16-bit system directory. There is no function that obtains the path of this directory, but it is searched. The name of this directory is System. 5. The Windows directory. Use the GetWindowsDirectory function to get the path of this directory. 6. The directories that are listed in the PATH environment variable. Each time the SetDllDirectory function is called, it replaces the directory specified in the previous SetDllDirectory call.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The new native path.

examples.label: Set to plugin folder:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

      <Calculation>MBS( "Process.SetDllDirectory"; "C:\Users\cs\AppData\Local\FileMaker\Extensions" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Process.SetFrontMost
functions.description: Moves FileMaker application to the front. You can use Process.IsFrontMost to check the status. You can use Window.Activate for Windows. Implemented Process.SetFrontMost for Windows in version 9.2. The plugin builds a list of all FileMaker windows. Then we activate the top most of them and move all other windows in z order behind.

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Process.SetQOSClass
functions.description: Sets the Quality Of Service class for the current thread. The Quality Of Service class for a thread tells the scheduler how to prioritize the thread and whether to run this on a faster or more efficient CPU core. MacOS can run threads marked as background on an efficient core to safe battery life. Linux does not allow to set higher priority (4 or 5) unless you have permissions to do so. Returns a native error code or OK in case of success.

parameters.name: QOS
parameters.description: The new QOS class. From highest level 5 down to background activity 1.

examples.label: Set QOS to background:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Process.SetQOSClass"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

This may run asynchronously, so you may do a script pause after it, so we have time to close the dialog.

Please do not call this function more than one time per second to avoid the progress window redraw to slow down your process.

parameters.name: Text

examples.label: Report progress:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetBottomText"; $index & " of " & $total & " files" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ProgressDialog.SetButtonCaption

parameters.name: Flag

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ProgressDialog.SetFont

parameters.name: FontName

functions.name: ProgressDialog.SetImage
functions.description: Sets the image. Please use container value as parameter. For best results use a PNG with alpha channel, so you don't see a border. Size should be 64 x 64 Pixels (72 dpi). If the picture does not fit, the plugin tries to scale it. Alpha Channel is not supported on Windows 2000, but works on Windows XP and newer. Internally we use GraphicsMagick, so all the image formats supported by GraphicsMagick should work. If there is no image, there is more room for your text in the dialog. Please use RGB picture. Other colorspaces like CMYK may not work.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: A container value with some picture. Can be JPEG, GIF, BMP, PNG or TIFF.

examples.label: Set Image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetImage"; Globals::ProgressLogo )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ProgressDialog.SetImageHeight
functions.description: Sets image view height. Sets the height for the image view showing the image. Default is 64 points. Image is stretched to this size on MacOS.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The new height.

functions.name: ProgressDialog.SetImageWidth
functions.description: Sets image view width. Sets the width for the image view showing the image. Default is 64 points. Image is stretched to this size on MacOS.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The new width.

functions.name: ProgressDialog.SetPosition

parameters.name: Y

parameters.name: Y

functions.name: ProgressDialog.SetPositionWindowCentered
functions.description: Set position of the dialog centered over a window. With X and Y you can move the dialog relative to the center of the window.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: Optional, Relative x coordinate.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: Optional, Relative y coordinate.

functions.name: ProgressDialog.SetProgress
functions.description: Sets the current progress value. Depending on your range, you need to multiply/divide to get into range from 0 to 100. Typically you can simply do math like this: $index * 100 / $total. That calculates the percentage of files processed if you have $total files and current file index is $index. If you process more than 100 items, please consider only updating progress if you actually moved forward 1%. You can do this by keeping variable with last progress you set and update only if new progress value is bigger. Please do not call this function more than one time per second to avoid the progress window redraw to slow down your process.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: New value in percent. Range from 0 to 100 for a normal progress bar or negative value for indeterminated progress bar.

examples.label: Set progress bar to indeterminate while preparing operations:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetProgress"; -1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Set later progress bar to current value:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetProgress"; $index * 100 / $total )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Update only when changed by one percent:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$percent
; Value:Round(100 *
$current
/
$total
; 0)]
If [
$percent
≠
$lastpercent
]
Set Variable [
$lastpercent
; Value:
$percent
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "ProgressDialog.SetProgress";
$percent
)]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Round(100 * $current / $total; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$percent</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$percent  ≠ $lastpercent</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$percent</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$lastpercent</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetProgress"; $percent )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ProgressDialog.SetTitleIcon
functions.description: Sets the icon of a window in Windows. You may need to use GMImage.NewImagesFromContainer to read image from container first.

parameters.name: ImageRef
parameters.description: The image reference for the picture image, a number returned from the GMImage.New functions

parameters.name: bigIcon
parameters.description: Pass 1 to set big icon (for app switcher) or 0 for small icon (icon in title bar). Default is 0.

examples.label: Set progress dialog icon:
examples.text_code:
# load image
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.NewImagesFromContainer
";
ProgressDialog::Icon
) ]
# set icon
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("ProgressDialog.SetTitleIcon";
$image
) ]
# later free it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
GMImage.Release
";
$image
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.NewImagesFromContainer"; ProgressDialog::Icon) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set icon</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.SetTitleIcon"; $image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> later free it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "GMImage.Release"; $image) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ProgressDialog.Update
functions.description: Redraws the progress dialog. When you change something on the dialog, it's marked for needing a redraw and this redraw is normally performed when there is some free CPU time like in a script pause. You can call this function to redraw now. Please do not call this function more than 10 times a second in your script. Else you may see a slowdown in script performance as redrawing the dialog takes time.

examples.label: Updates dialog with new button caption:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ProgressDialog.SetButtonCaption"; "Abbrechen" )
MBS( "ProgressDialog.Update" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Progressdialog.GetEvaluate
functions.description: Queries expression to evaluate.

functions.name: Progressdialog.GetFileName
functions.description: Queries file name.

functions.name: Progressdialog.GetScriptName
functions.description: Queries script name to run on button click.

functions.name: Progressdialog.SetEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression to evaluate on button click. You can set global variable to stop something or call MBS functions here.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to run. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

examples.label: Sets expression to hide dialog:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.SetEvaluate"; "MBS(\"ProgressDialog.Hide\")")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Progressdialog.SetScript
functions.description: Sets the script to run when button is clicked. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name for the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name to run. An empty name will disable the script calling.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The script parameter to pass.

examples.label: Set script to run
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ProgressDialog.SetScript"; Get(FileName); "CancelScript"; "optional parameter")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.CaptureCount
functions.description: Queries number of captured sub patterns.

parameters.name: RegExRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the regular expression returned from RegEx.Compile .

examples.label: Run query:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Pattern
; Value: "H(.*)o (.)or(..)" ]
Set Variable [
$Text
; Value: "Hello World Test" ]
# Compile
Set Variable [
$Regex
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Compile
";
$Pattern
; "CaseLess") ]
If [
MBS
("isError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$Regex
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
Set Variable [
$ExecuteResult
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Execute
";
$RegEx
;
$Text
; "") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Captures" ;
MBS
("RegEx.CaptureCount";
$RegEx
) & " captures" ]
End If
#  free last
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Release
";
$RegEx
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"H(.*)o (.)or(..)" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Pattern</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Hello World Test" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Compile</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Compile"; $Pattern; "CaseLess") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Regex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("isError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> $Regex </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Buttons>

  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.Compile
functions.description: Compiles a pattern. Returns the regular expression reference which you can than pass to other functions. Remember to use RegEx.Release to free memory later. You compile a pattern once. If you use RegEx.Execute several times on it, it may be worth using RegEx.Study for better performance. Please use \r instead of \n for the pattern as FileMaker uses Mac line endings. RegEx reference numbers are starting at 13000 and counting up for each new expression.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: The pattern to use.

parameters.name: CompilerOptions
parameters.description: A number for the options to use. e.g. 1 for case insensitive or 512 for ungreedy. Add 2 for multiline. Can be given as text or number.

examples.label: Compile with double escapement:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Compile"; "\\\""; 512+1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$regex</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find matches in multiline text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([
pattern = "^GR.";
text = "GR1¶GR2 ¶GR3 ";
CompileOptions = 2 /* multiline */ + 1 /* caseless */ + 4194304 /* newline any */;
ExecuteOptions = 4194304 /* newline any */;
regex = MBS("RegEx.Compile"; pattern; CompileOptions );
result = MBS( "RegEx.FindMatches"; regex; text; ExecuteOptions ; 0 );
r = MBS("RegEx.Release"; regex)
]; result )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Loop and find all matches for pattern:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$pattern
; Value: "(USt\-?ID:?|USt\-?IdNR\.?:?|Umsatzsteuer\-?ID:?|VAT\-?ID:?)[\s]?(DE)[\s]?([0-9]{9})" ]
Set Variable [
$ExecuteOptions
; Value: 0 ]
Set Variable [
$CompileOptions
; Value: "caseless" ]
Set Variable [
$regex
; Value:
MBS
("RegEx.Compile";
$Pattern
;
$CompileOptions
) ]
Set Variable [
$offset
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Execute
";
$regex
;
RegEx::Text
;
$ExecuteOptions
;
$offset
) ]
Exit Loop If [
$r
< 4 ]
Set Variable [
$s1
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$s2
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 2) ]
Set Variable [
$s3
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 3) ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$s1
& ¶ &
$s2
& ¶ &
$s3
]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Set Variable [
$offset
;
$offset
+ Value: GetAsNumber(GetValue(
MBS
("
RegEx.Results
";
$regex
); 2)) ]
End Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Release
";
$RegEx
) ]

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"(USt\-?ID:?|USt\-?IdNR\.?:?|Umsatzsteuer\-?ID:?|VAT\-?ID:?)[\s]?(DE)[\s]?([0-9]{9})" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pattern</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ExecuteOptions</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"caseless" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$CompileOptions</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Compile"; $Pattern; $CompileOptions) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$regex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$offset</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Execute"; $regex; RegEx::Text; $ExecuteOptions; $offset) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $r < 4 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Substring"; $RegEx; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Substring"; $RegEx; 2) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Substring"; $RegEx; 3) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s3</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> $s1 & ¶ & $s2 & ¶ & $s3 </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$offset + Value: GetAsNumber(GetValue(MBS("RegEx.Results"; $regex); 2)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$offset</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Release"; $RegEx) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.DataDetector
functions.description: Performs data detection to find details in text. Based on Apple's data detector framework, we can identify valuable information in texts. We return results as JSON array with an entry for each item found. All result items include the type, text, position and length. Date results include the Date, DateGMT, Duration and TimeZone entries. Date should be localized for FileMaker and DateGMT is standard SQL format for GMT. The address result may include Name, JobTitle, Organization, Street, City, State, ZIP and Country. You may run this from Windows or iOS with PSoS on a macOS server.

parameters.name: CheckingType
parameters.description: What type of data to look for. Can be a combination of Date, Address, Link, PhoneNumber or TransitInformation.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to check.
