functions.name: RegEx.Execute
functions.description: Executes a statement. You need to compile pattern before with RegEx.Compile function. Returns the number of results. Use RegEx.Results function to get list of results.

parameters.name: RegExRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the regular expression returned from RegEx.Compile .

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to search.

parameters.name: ExecuteOptions
parameters.description: The options for executing. Can be given as text or number.

parameters.name: Offset
parameters.description: Optional the offset in the text to start. Can be in range from 0 to length(text).

examples.label: Run query and show substrings:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Pattern
; Value: "H(.*)o (.)or(..)" ]
Set Variable [
$Text
; Value: "Hello World Test" ]
# Compile
Set Variable [
$Regex
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Compile
";
$Pattern
; "CaseLess") ]
If [
MBS
("isError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$Regex
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
Set Variable [
$ExecuteResult
; Value:
MBS
("RegEx.Execute";
$RegEx
;
$Text
; "") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Captures" ;
MBS
("
RegEx.CaptureCount
";
$RegEx
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Substrings" ;
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
) & ¶ &
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 1) & ¶ &
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 2) & ¶ &
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 3)]
End If
#  free last
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Release
";
$RegEx
) ]

Set Variable [
$pattern
; Value: "(USt\-?ID:?|USt\-?IdNR\.?:?|Umsatzsteuer\-?ID:?|VAT\-?ID:?)[\s]?(DE)[\s]?([0-9]{9})" ]
Set Variable [
$ExecuteOptions
; Value: 0 ]
Set Variable [
$CompileOptions
; Value: "caseless" ]
Set Variable [
$regex
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Compile
";
$Pattern
;
$CompileOptions
) ]
Set Variable [
$offset
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("RegEx.Execute";
$regex
;
RegEx::Text
;
$ExecuteOptions
;
$offset
) ]
Exit Loop If [
$r
< 4 ]
Set Variable [
$s1
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$s2
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 2) ]
Set Variable [
$s3
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 3) ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$s1
& ¶ &
$s2
& ¶ &
$s3
]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Set Variable [
$offset
;
$offset
+ Value: GetAsNumber(GetValue(
MBS
("
RegEx.Results
";
$regex
); 2)) ]
End Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Release
";
$RegEx
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"(USt\-?ID:?|USt\-?IdNR\.?:?|Umsatzsteuer\-?ID:?|VAT\-?ID:?)[\s]?(DE)[\s]?([0-9]{9})" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pattern</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ExecuteOptions</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"caseless" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$CompileOptions</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Compile"; $Pattern; $CompileOptions) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$regex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$offset</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Execute"; $regex; RegEx::Text; $ExecuteOptions; $offset) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $r < 4 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Substring"; $RegEx; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Substring"; $RegEx; 2) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Substring"; $RegEx; 3) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s3</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> $s1 & ¶ & $s2 & ¶ & $s3 </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$offset + Value: GetAsNumber(GetValue(MBS("RegEx.Results"; $regex); 2)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$offset</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Release"; $RegEx) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.Extract
functions.description: Extracts found text. Similar like RegEx.Replace , but returns only the found text given to the pattern for output.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to use.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: The pattern to use.

parameters.name: Rewrite
parameters.description: The output pattern. Use \ followed by number to access captures.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The PCRE options. Can be given as text or number.

examples.label: Extract phone number:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "RegEx.Extract"; "Call us at 01234/5678-90 to speak to sales."; "([0-9][0-9/\\-]+[0-9])"; "\1"; 1 )
Example result:
01234/5678-90
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RegEx.Extract"; "Call us at 01234/5678-90 to speak to sales."; "([0-9][0-9/\\-]+[0-9])"; "\1"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find a german IBAN in a text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
Input = "Hello DE29100100100987654321 World!";
R = MBS( "IBAN.RegEx"; "DE");
R = substitute(R; "^"; "");
R = substitute(R; "$"; "");

Found = MBS("RegEx.Extract"; input; R; "\\0"; 0 )
]; Found)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.FindMatches
functions.signature: MBS( "RegEx.FindMatches"; RegExRef; Text; ExecuteOptions { ; ReturnWholeMatch; NoReturnEnding } )
functions.description: Finds in a text all matches of a given pattern and returns list. Can be used for example to find tokens or placeholders in a text. If your search pattern has no captures, please pass 1 for ReturnWholeMatch or you won't see anything in result.

parameters.name: RegExRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the regular expression returned from RegEx.Compile .

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to search.

parameters.name: ExecuteOptions
parameters.description: The options for executing. Can be given as text or number.

parameters.name: ReturnWholeMatch
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return whole match including text you have in the pattern. Else pass 0 to get only the text from your captures.

parameters.name: NoReturnEnding
parameters.description: Pass 1 to have no extra newline character on the end of the returned list. Default is 0 to include one to easily concat lists.

examples.label: Search for numbers in text:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$regex
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Compile
"; "(\d+)\D"; 512+1)]
Set Variable [
$result
;
MBS
("RegEx.FindMatches";
$regex
; "aa 123 abc"; 0; 0)]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Release
";
$regex
)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Compile"; "(\d+)\D"; 512+1)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$regex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.FindMatches"; $regex; "aa 123 abc"; 0; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Release"; $regex)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find cd and 34 in the following text:
examples.text_code: Set Variable [ $regex ; Value: MBS (" RegEx.Compile "; "..(..).."; 512+1)] Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS ("RegEx.FindMatches"; $regex ; "abcdef¶123456"; 0; 0)] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" RegEx.Release "; $regex )]
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$regex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.FindMatches"; $regex; "<code>abcdef</code>¶<code>123456</code>"; 0; 0)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Release"; $regex)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find matches in multiline text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([
pattern = "^GR.";
text = "GR1¶GR2 ¶GR3 ";
CompileOptions = 2 /* multiline */ + 1 /* caseless */ + 4194304 /* newline any */;
ExecuteOptions = 4194304 /* newline any */;
regex = MBS("RegEx.Compile"; pattern; CompileOptions );
result = MBS( "RegEx.FindMatches"; regex; text; ExecuteOptions ; 0 );
r = MBS("RegEx.Release"; regex)
]; result )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.Match
functions.description: Matches a text against to a pattern. This compiles the pattern, executes it and looks if we have a match or not. Returns 1 if matches and 0 if not.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: The pattern to use.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to search.

parameters.name: CompilerOptions
parameters.description: A number for the options to use. e.g. 1 for case insensitive or 512 for ungreedy. Can be given as text or number.

parameters.name: ExecuteOptions
parameters.description: The options for executing. Can be given as text or number.

examples.label: Match Hello with pattern:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RegEx.Match"; "H(.*)o"; "Hello")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Match case less:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RegEx.Match"; "H(.*)o"; "hello"; "caseless")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Match multiline:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RegEx.Match"; "^Hello$"; "Test¶Hello¶World"; "MultiLine NewLineAny CaseLess"; "NewLineAny" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.MatchList
functions.description: Matches a list against a pattern. This compiles the pattern, executes it and looks if we have a match or not for each list item. Returns the list items which match.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: The pattern to use.

parameters.name: List
parameters.description: The list of texts to search.

parameters.name: CompilerOptions
parameters.description: A number for the options to use. e.g. 1 for case insensitive or 512 for ungreedy. Can be given as text or number.

parameters.name: ExecuteOptions
parameters.description: The options for executing. Can be given as text or number.

examples.label: Match Hello with pattern:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RegEx.MatchList"; "H(.*)o"; "Hello¶Test¶World¶House¶Test")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.MinimumLength
functions.description: Queries the minimum length of the text to match. Returns -1 if not applicable.

parameters.name: RegExRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the regular expression returned from RegEx.Compile .

functions.name: RegEx.NameCount
functions.description: Queries number of names.

parameters.name: RegExRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the regular expression returned from RegEx.Compile .

examples.label: Use a named group:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Pattern
; Value: "H(?P<MyName>.*)o" ]
Set Variable [
$Regex
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Compile
";
$Pattern
; "CaseLess") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Namecount" ;
MBS
( "RegEx.nameCount";
$RegEx
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

functions.name: RegEx.Quote
functions.description: Quotes text for use in patterns. Escapes all potentially meaningful regexp characters in text. The returned string, used as a regular expression, will exactly match the original string.  For example, "1.5-2.0?" may become: "1\.5\-2\.0\?". Note QuoteMeta behaves the same as perl's QuoteMeta function, *except* that it escapes the NUL character (\0) as backslash + 0, rather than backslash + NUL.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to quote.

examples.label: Escape a test string:
examples.text_code:
Show Custom Dialog [ "RegEx escaped" ;
MBS
( "RegEx.Quote"; "Hello? *test* 123" ) ]
Example result:
"Hello\?\ \*test\*\ 123"
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "RegEx escaped" ; MBS( "RegEx.Quote"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Hello? *test* 123" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.Replace
functions.signature: MBS( "RegEx.Replace"; Text; Pattern; Rewrite { ; Options; All; NthOffset } )
functions.description: Replace pattern once. You can replace the first match of pattern in text with rewrite pattern. Within rewrite pattern, backslash-escaped digits (\1 to \9) can be used to insert text matching corresponding parenthesized group from the pattern.  \0 in rewrite refers to the entire matching text.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to use.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: The pattern to use.

parameters.name: Rewrite
parameters.description: The output pattern.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The PCRE options. Can be given as text or number.

parameters.name: All
parameters.description: Whether to replace all occurances. Pass 1 to replace all or 0 to replace once. Default 0.

parameters.name: NthOffset
parameters.description: Number of occurrences to skip over on the start before doing replace. e.g. pass 1 to skip first match and start replacing at second one.

examples.label: Replace:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "RegEx.Replace"; "yabba dabba doo"; "b+"; "d" )
Example result:
"yadba dabba doo"
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RegEx.Replace"; "yabba dabba doo"; "b+"; "d" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Replace with multiline:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "RegEx.Replace"; "Hello¶World¶Testing"; "^World$"; "Welt"; "Multiline, NewlineAnyCRLF" )
Example result:
Hello
Welt
Testing
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "RegEx.Replace"; "Hello¶World¶Testing"; "^World$"; "Welt"; "Multiline, NewlineAnyCRLF" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

( "RegEx.Replace"; "John John John John"; "john"; "Thom"; "caseless";  0; 0 ) & ¶ &
MBS
( "RegEx.Replace"; "John John John John"; "john"; "Thom"; "caseless";  0; 1 ) & ¶ &
MBS
( "RegEx.Replace"; "John John John John"; "john"; "Thom"; "caseless";  0; 2 ) & ¶ &
MBS
( "RegEx.Replace"; "John John John John"; "john"; "Thom"; "caseless";  0; 3 )

Thom John John John
John Thom John John
John John Thom John
John John John Thom

MBS( "RegEx.Replace"; "John John John John"; "john"; "Thom"; "caseless";  0; 1 ) & ¶ &

MBS( "RegEx.Replace"; "John John John John"; "john"; "Thom"; "caseless";  0; 3 )</Calculation>
    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.ReplaceAll
functions.description: Replaces patterns. You can replace the all occurrences of pattern in text with rewrite pattern. Within rewrite pattern, backslash-escaped digits (\1 to \9) can be used to insert text matching corresponding parenthesized group from the pattern.  \0 in rewrite refers to the entire matching text. Replacements are not subject to re-matching.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to use.

parameters.name: Pattern
parameters.description: The pattern to use.

parameters.name: Rewrite
parameters.description: The output pattern.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: The PCRE options. Can be given as text or number.

examples.label: Replace all:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "RegEx.ReplaceAll"; "yabba dabba doo"; "b+"; "d" )

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.Results
functions.description: Queries the result from execute. The result are offsets into the text to search. Two for the whole match followed by 2 for each sub pattern.

parameters.name: RegExRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the regular expression returned from RegEx.Compile .

examples.label: Loop and find all matches for pattern:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$pattern
; Value: "(USt\-?ID:?|USt\-?IdNR\.?:?|Umsatzsteuer\-?ID:?|VAT\-?ID:?)[\s]?(DE)[\s]?([0-9]{9})" ]
Set Variable [
$ExecuteOptions
; Value: 0 ]
Set Variable [
$CompileOptions
; Value: "caseless" ]
Set Variable [
$regex
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Compile
";
$Pattern
;
$CompileOptions
) ]
Set Variable [
$offset
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Execute
";
$regex
;
RegEx::Text
;
$ExecuteOptions
;
$offset
) ]
Exit Loop If [
$r
< 4 ]
Set Variable [
$s1
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$s2
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 2) ]
Set Variable [
$s3
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Substring
";
$RegEx
; 3) ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$s1
& ¶ &
$s2
& ¶ &
$s3
]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Set Variable [
$offset
;
$offset
+ Value: GetAsNumber(GetValue(
MBS
("RegEx.Results";
$regex
); 2)) ]
End Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
RegEx.Release
";
$RegEx
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"(USt\-?ID:?|USt\-?IdNR\.?:?|Umsatzsteuer\-?ID:?|VAT\-?ID:?)[\s]?(DE)[\s]?([0-9]{9})" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$pattern</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ExecuteOptions</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"caseless" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$CompileOptions</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Compile"; $Pattern; $CompileOptions) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$regex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$offset</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Execute"; $regex; RegEx::Text; $ExecuteOptions; $offset) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $r < 4 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Substring"; $RegEx; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Substring"; $RegEx; 2) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Substring"; $RegEx; 3) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$s3</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> $s1 & ¶ & $s2 & ¶ & $s3 </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$offset + Value: GetAsNumber(GetValue(MBS("RegEx.Results"; $regex); 2)) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$offset</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("RegEx.Release"; $RegEx) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

=

$index

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.Substring
functions.description: Queries a substring. Index 0 gives whole match and index from 1 to RegEx.CaptureCount gives substrings matched.

parameters.name: RegExRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the regular expression returned from RegEx.Compile .

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index for the substring.

examples.label: Find seller ID in an URL:
examples.text_code:
Let ([
pattern = "^http.*\?.*&seller=(.*)$";
text = "https://www.example.com.com/gp/aag/main/ref=olp_merch_name_2?ie=UTF8&astn=B8J070KLDO&isFulfilled=1&seller=A2XKMLD9HIY1T";
regex =
MBS
( "
RegEx.Compile
"; pattern; 512+1 );
r =
MBS
( "
RegEx.Execute
"; regex; text; 0 );
r =
MBS
( "RegEx.Substring"; regex; 1 );
f =
MBS
( "
RegEx.Release
"; regex )
] ; r )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ([
pattern = "^http.*\?.*&seller=(.*)$";
text = "https://www.example.com.com/gp/aag/main/ref=olp_merch_name_2?ie=UTF8&astn=B8J070KLDO&isFulfilled=1&seller=A2XKMLD9HIY1T";

regex = MBS( "RegEx.Compile"; pattern; 512+1 );
r = MBS( "RegEx.Execute"; regex; text; 0 );
r = MBS( "RegEx.Substring"; regex; 1 );
f = MBS( "RegEx.Release"; regex )
] ; r )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: RegEx.SubstringList
functions.description: Returns list of substrings. First item in the list is the whole match, followed by other lines for each substring.

parameters.name: RegExRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the regular expression returned from RegEx.Compile .

functions.name: RegEx.Version
functions.description: Returns the version number of the PCRE library. e.g. "8.33 2013-05-28"

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SchemaChange.Changes
functions.description: Queries the changes, that the plugin received. Calling this function clears the list and reports it back as JSON array. The plugin adds two fields "counter" and "timestamp" with the change counter and the current timestamp as text. You may have a scheduled script running on the server to react to changes. e.g. by using JSON.InsertRecord to add this to a log table or by running Save as XML script step to export the solution. This function only works in the server scripting process in FileMaker 21.1 or newer. The catalog value in the JSON may report one of these catalogs: custom function, custom menu set, data source, extended privilege, field, layout, script, privilege set, table, table alias, theme, user account, value list. There may be more. Result is "" if no changes are recorded. You need to call SchemaChange.Enable to enable the feature.
functions.result: Returns JSON array, empty or error.

examples.label: Sample script to log changes to records:
examples.text_code:
# check for pending changes
Set Variable [
$changes
; Value:
MBS
("SchemaChange.Changes") ]
#
# if we have changes, log them to a table
If [ Length (
$changes
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
JSON.InsertRecords
"; Get(FileName); "SchemaChanges";
$changes
) ]
# table must have the following fields: account, catalog, file, id, user, basetableid, counter and time
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> check for pending changes</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("SchemaChange.Changes") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$changes</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> if we have changes, log them to a table</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( $changes ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("JSON.InsertRecords"; Get(FileName); "SchemaChanges"; $changes) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> table must have the following fields: account, catalog, file, id, user, basetableid, counter and time</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SchemaChange.Clear
functions.description: Clears the list of changes. Can be called after calling SchemaChange.Disable to clear memory from any pending changes.

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SchemaChange.Counter
functions.description: Queries the schema change counter. This increases every time the plugin receives a callback about a new change. This function can be used to quickly determinate if the counter changed and new changes are recorded. Counts up even if disabled, so you know how many changes we received even when we not collect them.

examples.label: Query last schema change counter:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Counter
; Value:
MBS
( "SchemaChange.Counter" ) ]
Example result:
123

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SchemaChange.Counter" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Counter</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ScreenshotRect
functions.signature: MBS( "ScreenshotRect"; Left; Top; Width; Height; Format; Quality; File Name )
functions.description: This function will take snapshot of the area of the screen described by the first four Parameters. Updated for macOS 10.7 in plugin version 2.5. Returns the image as container value, so you can assign it to a container field or save to disk with Container.WriteFile function.

parameters.name: Left
parameters.description: Left

parameters.name: Top
parameters.description: Top

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: Width of the rectangle

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: Height of Rectangle

parameters.name: Format
parameters.description: File Format. Currently only JPEG supported.

parameters.name: Quality
parameters.description: The quality of the image

parameters.name: File Name
parameters.description: The desired file name. The image is not written to disk. This file name is just added to the container for later export.

functions.result: Returns the image as a container value so you can assign it to a container.

examples.label: Capture a portion of the screen to a JPEG
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
/*-----------------PARAMETERS--------------------*/ 
Left = 100;
Top = 100;
Width = 200;
Height = 200;
Format = "JPEG";
Quality = 90;
File Name = "Test File.jpg"
];
/*-------------------FUNCTION----------------------*/ 
MBS( 
"ScreenshotRect";
Left ;
Top ;
Width ;
Height ;
Format ;
Quality ;
File Name 
)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Capture an area of screen as picture:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "ScreenshotRect"; 100; 100; 400; 300; "JPEG"; 90; "screenshot.jpg" ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="test "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebPolicyDelegate.AddMimeType
functions.description: Adds a mime type to the action list for the policy decisions.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: MIME Type as text.

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: The action you want to be made. This can be "use" when file should be viewed, "download" if file should be downloaded and "ignore" if file should be ignored.

examples.label: Makes sure all PDF files are downloaded:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebDownloadDelegate.Install" ; "web" )
MBS( "WebPolicyDelegate.AddMimeType"; "application/pdf"; "download" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Add a type to download:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebPolicyDelegate.AddMimeType"; "unknown/exe"; "download" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebPolicyDelegate.Install
functions.description: Assigns this WebViewer our policy delegate as a filter to the FileMaker built in delegate. When using our WebDownloadDelegate, you may see that it prefers to show a PDF with the build in PDF Viewer rather than downloading it. This set of functions serves to fix on problem: Getting the WebViewer to download files instead of showing them. So you can for example register that PDF files (mime type "application/pdf") are downloaded instead of viewed. All other policy things are forwarded to normal FileMaker policy delegate. And the plugin makes sure you don't install the policy delegate twice. Not supported for WebKit 2.x (FileMaker 16 Mac), but may work with plugin made web viewers (see WebView.Create ).

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

functions.result: Returns OK on success or error.

examples.label: Makes sure all PDF files are downloaded:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebDownloadDelegate.Install" ; "web" )
MBS( "WebPolicyDelegate.AddMimeType"; "application/pdf"; "download" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebPolicyDelegate.RemoveMimeType
functions.description: Removes a mime type to the action list for the policy decisions. When you remove all types you have the FileMaker default behavior. You can use type parameter "all" to remove all mime types.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: MIME Type as text.

functions.name: WebView.AddScriptMessageHandler
functions.description: Adds a script message handler. Adding a script message handler with name name causes the JavaScript function window.webkit.messageHandlers.name.postMessage(messageBody) to be defined in all frames in all web views that use the user content controller. Only for FileMaker 16 and newer. Trigger script in FileMaker from JavaScript: window.webkit.messageHandlers.test.postMessage({'filename':'WebView Message Handler.fmp12', 'parameter':'Hello World from JavaScript', 'scriptName':'test'}); Evaluate expression in JavaScript: window.webkit.messageHandlers.test.postMessage({'evaluate':'MBS( "Msgbox"; "Hello from Evaluate in JavaScript" )'}); Added iOS for version 9.5. New in version 10.3: If you pass "fm" as name, we connect the standard FileMaker handler, so you can restore it after you removed it earlier with WebView.RemoveScriptMessageHandler function.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the message handler.

examples.label: Add script handler:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Webview.AddScriptMessageHandler"; "web"; "test" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Restore FileMaker 19 handler:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Webview.AddScriptMessageHandler"; "web"; "fm" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.AddUserScript
functions.description: Adds a user script. Only for FileMaker 16 and newer in WebKit 2.x web viewer on Mac and iOS.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Source
parameters.description: The source code.

parameters.name: injectionTime
parameters.description: The time when the script should be injected into the webpage. AtDocumentStart: Inject the script after the document element is created, but before any other content is loaded. AtDocumentEnd: Inject the script after the document finishes loading, but before other subresources finish loading.

parameters.name: forMainFrameOnly
parameters.description: A Boolean value indicating whether the script should be injected only into the main frame (true) or into all frames (false).

examples.label: Add a test script:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Webview.AddUserScript"; "web"; "alert('Hello');"; "AtDocumentEnd"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.AllCookies
functions.description: Returns all website cookies as JSON. For WebKit 1.x, this returns global list of cookies. For WebKit 2.x, this returns list of cookies associated to the web viewer, which may be a persistent list. For WebKit 2.x we need MacOS 10.13 or iOS 11.0 to work. Windows support added for version 13.1. JSON objects have objects with name, domain, value, path, sessionOnly, HTTPOnly and expires with the time value in seconds. e.g. use TimeStamp ( Date ( 1 ; 1 ; 1970 ); Time ( 0; 0; 0 )) + $expires to calculate final timestamp on Windows. We added expires for macOS to have same value available.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

examples.label: Get all cookies:

Set Field [

;
MBS
("Webview.AllCookies"; "web") ]

,

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Webview.AllCookies"; "web") </Calculation>
    <Field table="WebView SelectedText" id="99999" name="Cookies "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.AllowsAnyHTTPSCertificateForHost
functions.description: Allows a certain host to allow use of any HTTP certificates. Sometimes you have to load a website where the SSL Zertifikate ist not for the given domain. With putting a domain on the exception list, we can allow loading the given site. Supported for WebKit 2.x (FileMaker 16 Mac) with MBS Plugin 9.3 or newer.

parameters.name: HostName
parameters.description: The hostname to allow.

examples.label: Allow some domains and load website:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Webview.AllowsAnyHTTPSCertificateForHost"; "macsw.de")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Webview.AllowsAnyHTTPSCertificateForHost"; "www.macsw.de")]
Set Web Viewer [Object Name: "web"; URL: "https://www.macsw.de/plugin/Prerelease/"]
examples.xml_code:

functions.name: WebView.CallFunction
functions.description: Calls a JavaScript function. Returns result as text or number. You can pass as many parameters as needed. Added iOS and macOS support for version 11.5. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Parameters parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: FunctionName
parameters.description: The function name.

parameters.name: Parameters...
parameters.description: Pass as many parameters as needed.

examples.label: Call with 2 parameters:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Webview.CallFunction"; "web"; "test"; "Hello"; "World")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.CanResetPageZoom
functions.description: Whether web page can be reset.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

functions.name: WebView.CanZoomPageIn
functions.description: Whether web page can be zoomed in. See also WebView.GetPageSizeMultiplier and WebView.SetPageSizeMultiplier functions.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

functions.name: WebView.CanZoomPageOut
functions.description: Whether web page can be zoomed out. See also WebView.GetPageSizeMultiplier and WebView.SetPageSizeMultiplier functions.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

functions.name: WebView.ClearBrowserSession
functions.description: Ends browser session. Flushes entries not in use from the password cache on the hard disk drive. Also resets the cache time used when the synchronization mode is once-per-session. This should clear current session cookies. For web viewer using Internet Explorer engine. If you close one by closing the window with web viewer control, this method should end session and a new window would start fresh again.

functions.name: WebView.ClearPrintParameters
functions.description: Clears the print parameters. Resets values like margins and paper format settings. This resets a few global variables in the MBS Plugin, which control the settings we apply when printing.

examples.label: Clear all printer settings:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.ClearPrintParameters")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.ClearSelection
functions.description: Clears selection. Not supported for FileMaker 16 on Mac, but works in FileMaker 9 to 15.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

functions.name: WebView.ClearVirtualHostNameToFolderMapping
functions.description: Clears a host name mapping for local folder. You define the mapping with WebView.SetVirtualHostNameToFolderMapping function. Requires FileMaker 19.4 and WebView2 browser component.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: HostName
parameters.description: The host name to be removed from the mapping.

examples.label: Create virtual host:

functions.name: WebView.ClickInput
functions.description: Clicks the specified Input Element.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: FormNameOrIndex
parameters.description: Either the Name of the Form or the Index starting at 0

parameters.name: InputNameOrIndex
parameters.description: Either the Name of the Button or the Index starting at 0

parameters.name: OnlyVisible
parameters.description: Whether to look for all fields or only the visible ones. Default is false.

examples.label: Load a website with login if needed:
examples.text_code:
# Load website
Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: "https://somedomain/search" ]
# Wait for it
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("
WebView.IsLoading
"; "web") ≠ 1 ]
End Loop
#
# Set login name
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.SetFormInputValue
"; "web"; "login_form"; "__ac_name"; "xxx") ]
If [
MBS
("iserror") ]
# No form, so we are already logged in?
If [ Position (
$r
; "failed to find form" ; 1 ; 1 ) = 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Fehler" ;
$r
]
End If
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
# Set password
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.SetFormInputValue
"; "web"; "login_form"; "__ac_password"; "yyy") ]
If [
MBS
("iserror") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Fehler" ;
$r
]
End If
# Wait
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
# Click login button
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.ClickInput"; "web"; "login_form"; "submit") ]
If [
MBS
("iserror") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Fehler" ;
$r
]
End If
# or submit form

; Value:

( "
WebView.FormSubmit

// If [
MBS
("iserror") ]
// Show Custom Dialog [ "Fehler" ;
$r
]
// End If
#
# Wait for page loading after login

Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("
WebView.IsLoading
"; "web") ≠ 1 ]
End Loop
# Go to search page where we want to be after login

functions.name: WebView.Close
functions.description: Closes a custom web view. Only for web viewer created with WebView.Create . Added iOS support for version 10.3.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

examples.label: Close WebViewer:
examples.text_code:
If [ Length(
$$web
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("Webview.Close";
$$web
) ]
Set Variable [
$$web
; Value: "" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ Length($$web) > 0 ] 
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS("Webview.Close"; $$web) ] 
	Set Variable [ $$web ; Value: "" ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.Cookies.ClearForURL
functions.description: Deletes all cookies for a given website. Returns number of deleted cookies. For WebKit 1.x, so only for web viewers created in FileMaker 15 or older or web viewers created with WebView.Create function.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The target URL of the website to use.

functions.name: WebView.Copy
functions.description: Copies current text selection to clipboard. This is like choosing the menu command to copy, but without using the menu. See also Clipboard.GetStyledText . As WebViewer functions often run asynchronously, you need to make a short script pause to let the WebViewer do the job.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

examples.label: Select all and copy:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Webview.SelectAll"; "web" ) & MBS( "Webview.Copy"; "web" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.CopyFormattedText
functions.description: Copies formatted text from web view to clipboard. You can insert it with the insert script step. Works well in FileMaker 12. In FileMaker 11 there are bugs so FileMaker doesn't import styles right from clipboard. Works for WebKit 2.x with plugin version 9.0. On windows, we do select all, copy and unselect to get all text to clipboard.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

functions.name: WebView.Create
functions.signature: MBS( "WebView.Create"; WindowRef; X; Y; W; H { ; Version; Flags } )
functions.description: Creates a new web view inside the window with the given rectangle. If you create WebKit 1.x view, you can use all the PDF/Image rendering functions, download delegate and callback functions, which don't work in WebKit 2.x. But if you use WebKit 2.x, you can use script message handler or user scripts, which are not supported in WebKit 1.x. The view is released automatically when window closes. Web viewers created via WebView.Create or WebView.CreateWithControl are independent of FileMaker, so they don't reload with a record change. They also don't print with the layout and stay if you change the layout. Added iOS support in version 10.3 using WebKit 2.x. Added Windows support in version 10.3 using IE engine. Please call WebView.Close later to close it. You can fmp: URLs and use FileMaker.PerformScript just like FileMaker's web viewer. For FileMaker.PerformScriptWithOption the option can be 1 for halt, 2 for exit, 3 for resume, 4 for pause. 0 is pause instead of continue and 6 is resume instead of Suspend and Resume since the plugin can't do these modes. e.g. trigger a script: FileMaker.PerformScript( "Script name", "Optional Parameter"); You can use FileMaker.Evaluate to directly evaluate something from JavaScript. e.g. query a get function like this: let filename = await FileMaker.Evaluate('Get(FileName)');

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The left coordinate in the window.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The top coordinate in the window.

parameters.name: W
parameters.description: The width of the drop area in the window.

parameters.name: H
parameters.description: The height of the drop area in the window.

parameters.name: Version
parameters.description: The WebKit version you need. Pass 1 for WebKit 1.x or 2 for WebKit 2.x. Default is zero, which prefers WebKit 1 on MacOS.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to allow triggering scripts from JavaScript. Pass 2 to allow FileMaker.Evaluate in JavaScript. Pass 3 to allow both. Default is 0. Mac only as not implemented for IE.

functions.result: Returns webviewer reference number or error.

examples.label: Create WebView:

Set Variable [
$x
; Value: 40 ]
Set Variable [
$y
; Value: 80 ]
Set Variable [
$w
; Value: 970 ]
Set Variable [
$h
; Value: 440 ]
Set Variable [
$$web
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.Create"; 0;
$x
;
$y
;
$w
;
$h
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.LoadURL
";
$$web
; "https://www.mbsplugins.de/filemaker") ]

    </Repetition>

      <Calculation>970 </Calculation>

    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$w</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>440 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$h</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>

functions.name: WebView.CreateWindow
functions.signature: MBS( "WebView.CreateWindow"; X; Y; Width; Height { ; Flags } )
functions.description: Creates a new window with a web viewer. The window is initially invisible, so you can finish setup and then call Window.Show function. The returned identifier can be used with our WebView functions as well as our Window functions. Uses WebView2 on Windows, just like FileMaker's WebViewer since version 19.3 and WebKit 2 for macOS. This WebView supports the fmp:// URLs to trigger scripts. You can use FileMaker.PerformScript just like FileMaker's web viewer. For FileMaker.PerformScriptWithOption the option can be 1 for halt, 2 for exit, 3 for resume, 4 for pause. 0 is pause instead of continue and 6 is resume instead of Suspend and Resume since the plugin can't do these modes. e.g. trigger a script: FileMaker.PerformScript( "Script name", "Optional Parameter"); You can use FileMaker.Evaluate to directly evaluate something from JavaScript. e.g. query a get function like this: let filename = await FileMaker.Evaluate('Get(FileName)');

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The x coordinate in points.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The y coordiate in points.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The new width of the window in points.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The new height of the window in points.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to allow triggering scripts from JavaScript. Pass 2 to allow FileMaker.Evaluate in JavaScript. Pass 3 to allow both. Default is 0.

examples.label: Create a web window:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$web
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.CreateWindow"; 200; 200; 500; 500) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebView.LoadURL
";
$web
; "https://mbsplugins.de/filemaker") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Window.SetTitle
";
$web
; "
MBS
Website") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Window.Show
";
$web
) ]

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.CreateWithControl
functions.signature: MBS( "WebView.CreateWithControl"; WindowRef; ControlName { ; Version; DX; DY; Flags } )
functions.description: Creates a new web view inside the window with the given placeholder control. If you create WebKit 1.x view, you can use all the PDF/Image rendering functions, download delegate and callback functions, which don't work in WebKit 2.x. But if you use WebKit 2.x, you can use script message handler or user scripts, which are not supported in WebKit 1.x. The view is released automatically when window closes. Web viewers created via WebView.Create or WebView.CreateWithControl are independent of FileMaker, so they don't reload with a record change. They also don't print with the layout and stay if you change the layout. You can fmp: URLs and use FileMaker.PerformScript just like FileMaker's web viewer. For FileMaker.PerformScriptWithOption the option can be 1 for halt, 2 for exit, 3 for resume, 4 for pause. 0 is pause instead of continue and 6 is resume instead of Suspend and Resume since the plugin can't do these modes. e.g. trigger a script: FileMaker.PerformScript( "Script name", "Optional Parameter"); You can use FileMaker.Evaluate to directly evaluate something from JavaScript. e.g. query a get function like this: let filename = await FileMaker.Evaluate('Get(FileName)');

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: ControlName
parameters.description: The name of the control on your FileMaker form. e.g. you can place a rectangle there to define the position.

parameters.name: Version
parameters.description: The WebKit version you need. Pass 1 for WebKit 1.x or 2 for WebKit 2.x. Default is zero, which prefers WebKit 1 on MacOS.

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move view more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move view more to top or bottom.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Pass 1 to allow triggering scripts from JavaScript. Pass 2 to allow FileMaker.Evaluate in JavaScript. Pass 3 to allow both. Default is 0. Mac only as not implemented for IE.

examples.label: Create Webviewer
examples.text_code:
#create with control on window
Set Variable [
$$web
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.CreateWithControl"; 0; "placeholder"; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.LoadURL
";
$$web
; "https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/") ]
examples.xml_code:

    </Repetition>

functions.name: WebView.Evaluate
functions.description: Evaluates a JavaScript expression. In contrast to WebView.RunJavaScript this function returns the result on Windows, but needs IE 9 or newer. Use WebView.SetInternetExplorerVersion to configure the IE version used in web viewer. For MacOS and iOS this passes through to WebView.RunJavaScript function. Returns result on MacOS/iOS if possible in original data type, so numbers and booleans are returned as number and not as text. We try to return array or objects as JSON. On Windows booleans and numbers are returned as numbers. For Windows with Edge browser or Web Direct, you may use the Perform JavaScript script step in FileMaker 19 instead.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The JavaScript expression to evaluate.

examples.label: Evaluate an expression:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.Evaluate"; "web"; "1+2" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

JSON.stringify(obj);")</Calculation>

</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Defines and runs a function in JavaScript in the web viewer:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.Evaluate"; "web"; 
"function makeCRCTable(){
        var c;
        var crcTable = [];
        for(var n =0; n < 256; n++){
                c = n;
                for(var k =0; k < 8; k++){
                        c = ((c&1) ? (0xEDB88320 ^ (c >>> 1)) : (c >>> 1));
                }
                crcTable[n] = c;
        }
        return crcTable;
}

function crc32(str) {
        var crcTable = crcTable || (crcTable = makeCRCTable());
        var crc = 0 ^ (-1);

        for (var i = 0; i < str.length; i++ ) {
                crc = (crc >>> 8) ^ crcTable[(crc ^ str.charCodeAt(i)) & 0xFF];
        }

        return (crc ^ (-1)) >>> 0;
};

crc32('Hello World');")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Fill a search form, submit and query result:
examples.text_code:
# load website with search field:
Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: "https://something.com/searchpage" ]
#
# wait for website to finish loading
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
Loop
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("
WebView.IsLoading
"; "web") ≠ 1 ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
End Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
#
# fill in search term and send change event, so this website sees it:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebView.RunJavaScript
"; "web"; "document.getElementById('searchField').value = '" & Substitute (
Web::SearchTerm
; "'" ; "" ) & "'") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebView.RunJavaScript
"; "web"; "var o = document.getElementById('searchField'); var evt = document.createEvent('Events'); evt.initEvent('change', true, true); o.dispatchEvent(evt); }") ]
#
# wait a bit
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
#
# send click event to search button
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebView.RunJavaScript
"; "web"; "var o = document.getElementById('searchButton'); var evt = document.createEvent('Events'); evt.initEvent('click', true, true); o.dispatchEvent(evt); ") ]
#
# wait for website to finish loading
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
Loop
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("
WebView.IsLoading
"; "web") ≠ 1 ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
End Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
#
# query values from website
Set Field [
Web::articleId
;
MBS
("WebView.Evaluate"; "web"; "document.getElementById('articleId').innerText;") ]
Set Field [
Web::price
;
MBS
("WebView.Evaluate"; "web"; "document.getElementById('price').innerText;") ]

examples.label: Wait for field named Remember to show up on website:
examples.text_code:
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.Evaluate"; "web"; "(null == document.getElementById('Remember'))") ]
Exit Loop If [
$r
= 0 ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
$count
+ 1 ]
If [
$count
= 20 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Timeout" ; "Failed to load website and get the remember checkbox." ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
End Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
# now you can set/query it
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.Evaluate"; "web"; "(null == document.getElementById('Remember'))") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $r = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$count + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count = 20 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Timeout" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Failed to load website and get the remember checkbox." </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>

    <Text> now you can set/query it</Text>
  </Step>

functions.name: WebView.FindByName
functions.description: Returns a unique ID that references a the web viewer. This function is used by all the other functions to target the correct web viewer. This function requires the WindowRef that is returned by the WebView.FindWindowByTitle function. Known Issues: In FileMaker 9, the WebView control must have the Display Progress Bar and Display Status Messages check boxes set to "unchecked" FileMaker 8,Windows PC only. The WebView Control can not be set to have "zero" line width on it's border. With the release of 1.4 this function became optional. You can now access a WebViewer by its name. But this is still useful in some scenarios. If you are trying to get a handle on WebViewer that is displayed in List mode, you must use this method. The WebViewer reference is only valid until FileMaker destroys the WebViewer. So better search again when you move to a new record or change the window. The WebViewer must have content to be found. If you don't load a page, you should still put an "about:blank" URL there. You may need to have a script pause for FileMaker to create the web viewer after a layout change. If you use Freeze Window command, the layout may not be drawn and thus the plugin may not find the web viewer.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: WebViewerObjectName
parameters.description: The name of the Web Viewer as set in the object info palette.

examples.label: Find the Web View ID
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
WebViewerName = "web";
WindowRef = MBS("Window.FindByTitle"; Get(WindowName))
];

MBS("WebView.FindByName"; WindowRef; WebViewerName)

)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find Webviewer in current window:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.FindByName"; Get(WindowName); "web")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.FindWindowByIndex
functions.description: Returns the Window Reference for a window specified by the Index parameter. The Window Reference is a unique ID handed out by the operating system. It is used by the rest of the WebView functions Caution: Passing 0 for Index will grab the front most window which should be your FileMaker window, but it could be any window including dialogs. This is a particular problem with the "Data Viewer" which is always in the front. Window.FindByTitle was added to version 1.1 as a more reliable way of getting the Window.Ref. The window reference is only valid until the window is closed.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: any number starting with 0

functions.name: WebView.FindWindowByTitle
functions.description: Returns the Window Reference for a window specified by the Index parameter. The Window Reference is a unique ID. The window reference is only valid until the window is closed. On normal FileMaker on desktop you can use Get(WindowName), but on FileMaker Server the name includes the server name, so you need to use: Get(WindowName) & " (" & Get(HostName) & ")" If 0 is returned, no window was found.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title of the widow

functions.result: Returns window reference number or error.

examples.label: Get the Window Reference of the current window
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.FindWindowByTitle" ; Get(WindowName) )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Find window reference on FileMaker Server where FileMaker includes server name in window name:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.FindWindowByTitle"; Get(WindowName) & " (" & Get(HostName) & ")")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

Field = "name";
Form = "myForm";
WebViewerName = "MyWebViewer";

WindowRef = MBS("Window.FindByTitle"; Get(WindowName));
WebViewerID = MBS("WebView.FindByName"; WindowRef; WebViewerName)

];


MBS("WebView.GetFormValue"; WebViewerID; Form; Field)

)</Calculation>

functions.name: WebView.GetHTMLText
functions.description: Queries the current HTML of the document. This is not the HTML code loaded from the server, but the current state. Typically the website is modified with Javascript to add widgets or hide/show things at runtime. For websites with frames, result may only contain the main frame and not any child frames. See also WebView.GetPlainText , WebView.GetFormattedText and WebView.SelectedText . Alternative you can run JavaScript to query document.body.parentElement.outerHTML yourself. On Windows, the browser may include header lines in front of the html with meta data. Our function can only get the HTML in the DOM tree. Anything in an IFRAME is not captured. Also anything generated on the fly in JavaScript may not show up, unless it is linked to the DOM tree.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

functions.result: Returns html code as text or error.

examples.label: Query plain text from WebViewer:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

functions.name: WebView.GetPlainText
functions.description: Queries the current text of the document. No formatting, but only the text. This may show more/less text than you see. For websites with frames, result may only contain the main frame and not any child frames. See also WebView.GetHTMLText , WebView.GetFormattedText and WebView.SelectedText . Alternative you can run JavaScript to query document.body.parentElement.outerText yourself. Due to a bug in IE on Windows, you may see CSS in the text returned. Our function can only get the HTML in the DOM tree. Anything in an IFRAME is not captured. Also anything generated on the fly in JavaScript may not show up, unless it is linked to the DOM tree.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

examples.label: Wait for some text to show up on website:
examples.text_code:
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.GetPlainText"; "web") ]
Exit Loop If [ Position (
$text
; "following quotes"; 1; 1 ) > 1 ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
$count
+ 1 ]
If [
$count
= 60 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Timeout" ; "Failed to load website and get the quotes text." ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
End Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.GetPlainText"; "web") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> Position ( $text; "following quotes"; 1; 1 ) > 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$count + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count = 60 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Timeout" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Failed to load website and get the quotes text." </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.GetPreferences
functions.description: Queries WebView preferences parameters. For WebKit 1.x you can pass empty WebViewerRef to access global preferences. Available keys for WebKit 1.x: allowsAnimatedImageLooping, allowsAnimatedImages, cacheModel, cursiveFontFamily, defaultFixedFontSize, defaultFontSize, defaultTextEncodingName, fantasyFontFamily, fixedFontFamily, javaEnabled, javaScriptCanOpenWindowsAutomatically, javaScriptEnabled, loadsImagesAutomatically, minimumFontSize, minimumLogicalFontSize, plugInsEnabled, privateBrowsingEnabled, sansSerifFontFamily, serifFontFamily, shouldPrintBackgrounds, standardFontFamily, tabsToLinks, userStyleSheetEnabled or usesPageCache. Available keys for WebKit 2.x (FileMaker 16): developerExtrasEnabled, logsPageMessagesToSystemConsoleEnabled, minimumFontSize, javaScriptEnabled, javaScriptCanOpenWindowsAutomatically, telephoneNumberDetectionIsEnabled, javaEnabled and plugInsEnabled. New in version 10.2: mediaDevicesEnabled and mediaStreamEnabled to allow camera/microphone/screen access. New in version 10.3: CrossOriginResourcePolicyEnabled and webSecurityEnabled to enable cross site scripting. Please check WebKit documentation for details. Implemented for Windows with Microsoft Edge/Chrome engine in MBS Plugin 11.5 when using FileMaker Pro 19.4 or newer. Possible keys: IsScriptEnabled, IsWebMessageEnabled, AreDefaultScriptDialogsEnabled, IsStatusBarEnabled, AreDevToolsEnabled, AreDefaultContextMenusEnabled, AreHostObjectsAllowed, IsZoomControlEnabled, IsBuiltInErrorPageEnabled, areBrowserAcceleratorKeysEnabled, IsPinchZoomEnabled, IsPasswordAutosaveEnabled and IsGeneralAutofillEnabled. MBS Plugin 11.5 adds fraudulentWebsiteWarningEnabled for macOS 10.15 and iOS 13.0 to check whether the warnings or fraudulent websites is enabled. The textInteractionEnabled setting in macOS 12.0 and iOS 15.0 allows to check whether text interactions are enabled. Added IsSwipeNavigationEnabled and HiddenPdfToolbarItems options in version 14.2 for Windows using WebView 2.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: ParameterName
parameters.description: The name of the parameter.

functions.result: Returns a boolean value for allowsAnimatedImageLooping, allowsAnimatedImages, javaEnabled, javaScriptCanOpenWindowsAutomatically, javaScriptEnabled, loadsImagesAutomatically, plugInsEnabled, privateBrowsingEnabled, shouldPrintBackgrounds, tabsToLinks, userStyleSheetEnabled and usesPageCache; a number for cacheModel, defaultFixedFontSize, defaultFontSize, minimumFontSize or minimumLogicalFontSize; a string for cursiveFontFamily, defaultTextEncodingName, fantasyFontFamily, fixedFontFamily, sansSerifFontFamily, serifFontFamily or standardFontFamily.
