functions.name: WebView.GetPrintParameter
functions.description: Queries print parameters. Returns the values you set previously with WebView.SetPrintParameter function. Parameters for iOS: Duplex: None, LongEdge or ShortEdge, OutputType: General, Photo, Grayscale or PhotoGrayscale, JobName and Orientation: Portrait or Landscape. Parameters for Windows with Edge: Orientation: Portrait or Landscape. ShouldPrintBackgrounds, PaperWidth, PaperHeight, BottomMargin, TopMargin, LeftMargin, RightMargin, ShouldPrintHeaderAndFooter: 1 or 0, ShouldPrintSelectionOnly: 1 or 0, HeaderTitle and FooterUri Parameters for macOS with WebKit 2: HorizontallyCentered, VerticallyCentered, ShowPrintPanel, Orientation: Portrait or Landscape. PaperName, Printer, PaperWidth, PaperHeight, BottomMargin, TopMargin, LeftMargin, RightMargin, ShouldPrintBackgrounds and MinimumFontSize.

parameters.name: ParameterName
parameters.description: The name of the parameter. For MacOS, can be horizontallyCentered, verticallyCentered, showPrintPanel, orientation, printer, bottomMargin, topMargin, leftMargin or rightMargin.

functions.result: Returns a boolean value for horizontallyCentered, verticallyCentered and showPrintPanel. Returns "portrait" or "landscape" for orientation. Returns name for printer. Returns a number for bottomMargin, topMargin, leftMargin or rightMargin.

For some media the data is not available for the plugin, so you can query the URL using WebView.GetURL and download it yourself.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.HasOnlySecureContent
functions.description: Queries whether website is secure. Returns a boolean value indicating whether all resources on the page have been loaded over securely encrypted connections. On Windows and older WebKit 1.x, just returns whether URL is https.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

functions.name: WebView.HasParent
functions.description: Checks whether WebView has parent control. When you use custom WebViewer with our plugin, you may run into the situation, that FileMaker rebuilds the layout (e.g. after changing a value list!). Then WebView.HasParent will return 0 and you know the WebViewer got removed by FileMaker. So when you have a script triggered by user going to new layout, you can check the status of web viewer and if needed rebuild it. Or change workflows, so if user needs to change a value list, they may need to open a card window then reach the edit dialog and when they click back button to close card, you can reload WebViewer.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

examples.label: Whether browser has a parent:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.HasParent"; $$web) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$hasParent</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.InstallCallback
functions.description: Installs JavaScript callback into web viewer. This function needs to be called one time to enable JavaScript to reach out from the web viewer to run a FileMaker Script. One strategy is to call this function on start-up before any WebViewers have been displayed. To call back from Javascript to FileMaker, you assign a string to the window.status property: window.status="Filename:Scriptname" or window.status="Filename:Scriptname:Parameter" The Parameter part can contain ":". Filename must include file extensions so FileMaker finds the database file. You can also call it within a link with "javascript:window.status='Filename:Scriptname:Parameter'". Like this: href="javascript:window.status='callbacks.fp7:Another Script:Hello World'". Does not work currently with Internet Explorer 10 on Windows. Not supported for WebKit 2.x (FileMaker 16 Mac), but works in FileMaker 16, when using a Web Viewer created using WebView.Create . For FileMaker 16, please check WebView.AddScriptMessageHandler function as a replacement.

examples.label: Install Callbacks
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.InstallCallback")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.InstallMultiFileOpenDialog
functions.description: Installs support to the WebViewer for multiple file selections. Call once when solution opens to have all web viewer allow multiple selections on webpages which support that. Added support for FileMaker 16 or newer in plugin version 7.1.

functions.name: WebView.InstallUserMediaAccess
functions.description: Enables user media access. For FileMaker Pro on macOS or FileMaker iOS SDK applications on iOS. Call once on start of FileMaker to enable user media access. See AVRecorder.AuthorizationStatusForMediaType to query status for camera/microphone access.

parameters.name: Devices
parameters.description: Which devices to enable. Pass 1 for microphone. Pass 2 for camera. Pass 4 for display. For multiple values, just add them together.

examples.label: Installs it, enable in preferences and load an URL:
examples.text_code:
# Make sure it's installed
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.InstallUserMediaAccess"; 1+2+4) ]
# make sure you have a web viewer with about:blank as URL
# enable via preferences
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.SetPreferences
"; "web"; "mediaStreamEnabled"; 1 ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.SetPreferences
"; "web"; "mediaDevicesEnabled"; 1 ) ]
# load some website
Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: "https://test.webrtc.org" ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Make sure it's installed</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.InstallUserMediaAccess"; 1+2+4) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> make sure you have a web viewer with about:blank as URL</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> enable via preferences</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetPreferences"; "web"; "mediaStreamEnabled"; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetPreferences"; "web"; "mediaDevicesEnabled"; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> load some website</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="146" name="Set Web Viewer">
    <Action value="GoToURL"/>
    <ObjectName>
      <Calculation>"web"</Calculation>
    </ObjectName>
    <URL custom="False">

    </URL>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.IsLoading
functions.description: This function returns True if the Specified web viewer is still loading. This useful if you need to delay making a javascript call to WebViewer until it it is done loading. If you use this in a loop to wait for WebViewer, please exit loop when this function doesn't return a 1. So you exit also when an error occurs. For websites which load more data (e.g. pictures) while you surf the page, the plugin will not notice that.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

examples.label: Check loading state for WebViewer named web
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.IsLoading"; "web")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Wait for WebViewer to finish loading
examples.text_code:
# first give FileMaker some time to load
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,3]
# now wait until it is fully finished
Loop
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("WebView.IsLoading"; "web") ≠ 1]
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,1]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> first give FileMaker some time to load</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,3</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now wait until it is fully finished</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>MBS("WebView.IsLoading"; "web") ≠ 1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.LoadURL

parameters.name: URL

parameters.name: cachePolicy

parameters.name: timeoutInterval

functions.name: WebView.MoveFocus
functions.description: Moves the focus. WebView will get focus and focus will be set to correspondent element in the page hosted in the WebView. For Programmatic reason, focus is set to previously focused element or the default element if no previously focused element exists. For Next reason, focus is set to the first element. For Previous reason, focus is set to the last element. WebView changes focus through user interaction including selecting into a WebView or Tab into it. For tabbing, the app runs MoveFocus with Next or Previous to align with Tab and Shift+Tab respectively when it decides the WebView is the next tabable element.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Reason
parameters.description: The reason to move focus. Pass Programmatic, Next or Previous.

functions.name: WebView.PostURL
functions.description: Navigates to a resource identified by a URL or to a file identified by a full path. This function is similar to WebView.LoadURL , but can pass additional headers or POST data. For MacOS and iOS, please use WebView.SetCustomUserAgent to overwrite user agent.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: URL
parameters.description: The URL, full path, or Universal Naming Convention (UNC) location and name of the resource to display.

parameters.name: PostData
parameters.description: The data to post. The post data that is sent to the server as part of a HTTP POST transaction. A POST transaction is typically used to send data gathered by an HTML form. If this parameter does not specify any post data, this method issues an HTTP GET transaction. This parameter is ignored if the URL is not an HTTP URL.

parameters.name: Headers
parameters.description: The text list that contains additional HTTP headers to send to the server. These headers are added to the default headers. For example, headers can specify the action required of the server, the type of data being passed to the server, or a status code. This parameter is ignored if the URL is not an HTTP URL.
parameters.example: "Test: MyHeader 123 User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_14_6) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/13.0.4 Safari/605.1.15"

examples.label: Load URL in WebViewer by doing a POST with custom header to overwrite user agent:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.PostURL"; "web"; 
// URL
"https://www.monkeybreadsoftware.com/filemaker/echo.cgi"; 
// some POST data
"Hello World"; 
// and our custom headers
"Test: MyHeader 123¶User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (FileMaker; Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_14_6) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/13.0.4 Safari/605.1.15 )")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: WebView.PrintToFile
functions.description: Prints the current web view content to PDF file. You can get/set some parameters on macOS using the WebView.GetPrintParameter / WebView.SetPrintParameter functions and WebView.SetPreferences function. Especially you can control whether to print background or not. If needed you can import this PDF file with Files.ReadPDF into a container file. See also WebView.RenderPDF . Supported for WebKit 2.x (FileMaker 16 Mac) with version 11.5. Windows support added for version 13.1 for FileMaker Pro 19.4 or newer for WebView2 control. Doesn't work with the Internet Explorer, e.g. with WebView.Create . This usually does not work if the web viewer shows a PDF file. The web content is downloaded by a background process where the plugin has no direct influence to get the PDF and print it. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: Native file path where to save PDF.

examples.label: Create PDF and import it:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Folders.UserTemporary
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$path
; "website.pdf" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.PrintToFile";
$$web
;
$path
) ]
Set Field [
Own WebView::Container
;
MBS
( "
Files.ReadPDF
";
$path
) ]
examples.xml_code:

    </Repetition>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.PrintToFile"; $$web; $path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">

  </Step>

functions.name: WebView.Reload

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Force
parameters.description: Whether to ignore cache and reload from website. Default is 0 for normal reloading which may use cached content.

functions.name: WebView.RemoveAllUserScripts

functions.name: WebView.RemoveScriptMessageHandler
functions.description: Removes a script message handler. Only for FileMaker 16 and newer. Added iOS for version 9.5. This can be used to remove the standard message handler from FileMaker.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef

functions.name: WebView.RenderImage
functions.description: Renders the WebViewer to a picture. Added Windows support in version 3.0. Due the way we capture image on Windows (we ask WebViewer to print to image), some things do not show up in the image. In that case, please use WebView.Screenshot function. On Mac you can use WebView.RenderPDF to get a PDF of the website. For macOS the image size is the size of the full website. On Windows we only capture the area of the WebViewer, so you may see scrollbars. For macOS please use WebView.SetPreferences to set whether to include background images. Added support for macOS 10.13 in FileMaker 16 or newer to render webpage in v7.4. Works in FileMaker 15 and older in all MacOS versions. Works in all FileMaker Mac versions, when using a Web Viewer with WebKit 1.x created using WebView.Create . Fixed for iOS for 8.2 release. On Windows seems not to work well in latest IE versions. so you may better use WebView.Screenshot .

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.result: Returns a container with a JPEG picture of the webpage. You can store it in a media field.

examples.label: Render Image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.RenderImage"; $$web; "PNG"; "webview.png") </Calculation>
    <Field table="Own WebView" id="99999" name="Container "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.RenderPDF
functions.description: Renders the web view to a PDF. For OS X please use WebView.SetPreferences to set whether to include background images. If you need a multi page PDF file, please use WebView.PrintToFile function. Implemented for WebKit 2 in MBS Plugin 10.5 for macOS with Safari 14.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

parameters.name: DontRenderPreview
parameters.description: Pass 1 to not create a JPEG with preview of first page as part of the container.

functions.result: Returns a container with a PDF of the webpage (and a JPEG preview). You can store it in a media field.

examples.label: Render PDF:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.RenderPDF"; $$web; "webview.pdf") </Calculation>
    <Field table="Own WebView" id="99999" name="Container "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.ResetPageZoom
functions.description: Resets the web page zoom.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

functions.name: WebView.RunJavaScript
functions.description: Executes the Javascript on the specified webViewer control. Allows you to call any Javascript function that is in loaded in the WebViewer OR you can just run arbitrary JavaScript on an empty web page. The results of the JavaScript are returned on Macintosh only. There is another function called WebView.RunJavaScriptReturnTitle which can be used to return the value of a Javascript function on both platforms. Use "\n" in javascript strings for a newline character. Does not work on Web Direct! See newer WebView.Evaluate function which works better on Windows and returns results. Check JS Functions for JavaScript engine for cross platform code to run without web viewer. Version 10.0 or newer return result on MacOS/iOS if possible in original data type, so numbers are returned as number and not as text. We try to return array or objects as JSON. For Windows with Edge browser or Web Direct, you may use the Perform JavaScript script step in FileMaker 19 instead.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: JavaScript
parameters.description: The Javascript to be executed

parameters.name: Async
parameters.description: Whether to run asynchronously. On MacOS and iOS you can choose between running sync and waiting for result (and returns result) or running asnychronously and returning just OK. For older WebKit 1.x in FileMaker 15 or older or web viewers created with WebView.Create function, we always use synchronous mode. Windows is always synchronously. Default is 0 for synchronous mode.

examples.label: Set the htmlcode of a field as string:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.RunJavaScript"; "browser"; "document.getElementById('td_ProcName_v').innerHTML='hello';" )</Calculation>

examples.label: Run JavaScript
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[

// very simple javascript

javascript = "alert('OK');";

webViewerName = "myWebViewer";
windowRef = MBS("Window.FindByTitle"; Get ( WindowName ));
webViewerID = MBS("WebView.FindByName"; windowRef; webViewerName)

];
Case(
webViewerID = 0; "Web Viewer \"" & webViewerName & "\" Not Found: " &windowRef ;

MBS("WebView.RunJavaScript"; webViewerID; javaScript)

)

)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Send change event to send to a field with ID $FieldID so javascript code on webpage knows we changed something:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.RunJavaScript" ; "web"; 
"var o = document.getElementById('" & $FieldID & "'); 
        var evt = document.createEvent('Events'); 
        evt.initEvent('change', true, true); 
        o.dispatchEvent(evt); ")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Login for realtytrac website:
examples.text_code:
Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: "https://www.realtytrac.com/login" ]
#
# wait for website loading
#
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): .1 ]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("
WebView.IsLoading
"; "web") ≠ 1 ]
End Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): .1 ]
#
# fill in form values with login:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.SetFormInputValue
" ; "web"; 0; "userName"; "xxx" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.SetFormInputValue
" ; "web"; 0; "password"; "yyy" ) ]
#
# send change events to let the javascript framework know we changed them
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.RunJavaScript" ; "web"; "var o = document.getElementById('loginFormPagePassword');       if (document.createEvent)
{
var evt = document.createEvent('Events');
evt.initEvent('change', true, true);
o.dispatchEvent(evt);
}")]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.RunJavaScript" ; "web"; "var o = document.forms[0].userName.value;
if (document.createEvent)
{
var evt = document.createEvent('Events');
evt.initEvent('change', true, true);
o.dispatchEvent(evt);
}")]
#
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): .1 ]
#
# Send click event to submit button
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.RunJavaScript" ; "web"; "var o = document.getElementById('btnLoginPageSubmit');            if (document.createEvent)
{
var evt = document.createEvent('Events');
evt.initEvent('click', true, true);
o.dispatchEvent(evt);
}")]

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

      <Calculation>"https://www.realtytrac.com/login" </Calculation>
    </URL>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

    <Restore state="False"/>

  </Step>

    <Calculation>.1 </Calculation>
  </Step>

  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> fill in form values with login:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetFormInputValue" ; "web"; 0; "userName"; "xxx" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetFormInputValue" ; "web"; 0; "password"; "yyy" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.RunJavaScript" ; "web"; "var o = document.forms[0].userName.value;if (document.createEvent)     {        var evt = document.createEvent('Events');         evt.initEvent('change', true, true);         o.dispatchEvent(evt);      }")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">

examples.label: Go to record, wait and than jump to position to play video from there: (Mac)
examples.text_code:
# Go to record with video
Go to Record/Request/Page [ Next ; Exit after last: Off ]
# give time to load movie
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
# jump to position
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.RunJavaScript"; "MovieView"; "document.getElementById('fm-player').currentTime = 30;" ) ]
# play
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.RunJavaScript"; "MovieView"; "document.getElementById('fm-player').play();" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Go to record with video</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="16" name="Go to Record/Request/Page">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Exit state="False"/>
    <RowPageLocation value="Next"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> give time to load movie</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,5 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> jump to position</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.RunJavaScript"; "MovieView"; "document.getElementById('fm-player').currentTime = 30;" ) </Calculation>

examples.label: Work with form fields via JavaScript:

MBS( "WebView.RunJavaScript" ; "web"; "document.forms[0].elements[0].name")
// Query name of first element in first form:

MBS( "WebView.RunJavaScript" ; "web"; "document.forms[0].elements[0].value")
// Query value of first element in first form:

MBS( "WebView.RunJavaScript" ; "web"; "document.forms['RegisterForm'].elements['path'].value")
// Query value of element by name in form by name:

// You can mix index or field/form names as needed.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Fill a search form, submit and query result:
examples.text_code:
# load website with search field:
Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: "https://something.com/searchpage" ]
#
# wait for website to finish loading
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
Loop
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("
WebView.IsLoading
"; "web") ≠ 1 ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
End Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
#
# fill in search term and send change event, so this website sees it:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.RunJavaScript"; "web"; "document.getElementById('searchField').value = '" & Substitute (
Web::SearchTerm
; "'" ; "" ) & "'") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.RunJavaScript"; "web"; "var o = document.getElementById('searchField'); var evt = document.createEvent('Events'); evt.initEvent('change', true, true); o.dispatchEvent(evt); }") ]
#
# wait a bit
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
#
# send click event to search button
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.RunJavaScript"; "web"; "var o = document.getElementById('searchButton'); var evt = document.createEvent('Events'); evt.initEvent('click', true, true); o.dispatchEvent(evt); ") ]
#
# wait for website to finish loading
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
Loop
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("
WebView.IsLoading
"; "web") ≠ 1 ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
End Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
#
# query values from website
Set Field [
Web::articleId
;
MBS
("
WebView.Evaluate
"; "web"; "document.getElementById('articleId').innerText;") ]
Set Field [
Web::price
;
MBS
("
WebView.Evaluate
"; "web"; "document.getElementById('price').innerText;") ]

examples.label: Send change, input and blur events to text field:
examples.text_code:
# define sendChangeEvent function
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.RunJavaScript"; "web"; " function sendChangeEvent(ID) {  var o = document.getElementById(ID);  var evt = document.createEvent('Events');  evt.initEvent('change', true, true);  o.dispatchEvent(evt); } ") ]
#
# define sendInputEvent function
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.RunJavaScript"; "web"; " function sendInputEvent(ID) {  var o = document.getElementById(ID);  var evt = document.createEvent('Events');  evt.initEvent('input', true, true);  o.dispatchEvent(evt); } ") ]
#
# define sendBlurEvent function
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.RunJavaScript"; "web"; " function sendBlurEvent(ID) {  var o = document.getElementById(ID);  var evt = document.createEvent('Events');  evt.initEvent('blur', true, true);  o.dispatchEvent(evt); } ") ]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.RunJavaScript"; "web"; "document.getElementById('name').value = '" & Substitute (
$Name
; "'" ; "" ) & "'") ]
#
# send three events as we often don't know which one is used on the website to recognize the change
#
# send input event, so JavaScript code notices we put something in
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.RunJavaScript"; "web"; "sendInputEvent('name');") ]
# send change event, so JavaScript code notices we changed text
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.RunJavaScript"; "web"; "sendChangeEvent('name');") ]
# send blur event, so JavaScript code notices the user left the field
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.RunJavaScript"; "web"; "sendBlurEvent('name');") ]
# give time for web viewer to run all the events
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 ]

functions.name: WebView.RunJavaScriptReturnTitle
functions.description: Run the specified JavaScript and then returns the value of the title property to FileMaker. To return a result of the JavaScript to FileMaker, simply set "document.title" to the return value. Why did we do this? Because it was the way we could get the result back from the web view on windows. Use "\n" in javascript strings for a newline character. See newer WebView.Evaluate function which works better on Windows and returns results. Check JS Functions for JavaScript engine for cross platform code to run without web viewer. For Windows with Edge browser or Web Direct, you may use the Perform JavaScript script step in FileMaker 19 instead.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: JavaScript
parameters.description: the text source of the javascript

functions.name: WebView.Screenshot
functions.description: Takes a screenshot of the area used by the WebViewer. The plugin offers several methods. WebView.RenderImage works fine on Mac, but not always on Windows. WebView.RenderPDF is Mac only. So especially for websites with flash or other content which can't be printed, the screenshot method is the best one. (older versions before 5.2 would have an unused extra parameter before ImageType) Windows only supports JPEG as type. Added iOS support for version 8.5. On Windows may get wrong rectangle if Windows runs desktop with 200% resolution, but you have a 100% resolution screen. For Windows and FileMaker 19.3, you can use Container.Screenshot function instead.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.result: Returns image for storing in container.

$image
]

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Webview.Scrollbar.getVerticalPosition"; "web" ) & " of " & MBS( "Webview.Scrollbar.getVerticalMaximum"; "web" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.Scrollbar.getVerticalPosition
functions.description: Queries current value for vertical scrollbar. Alternative you can run JavaScript to query document.scrollingElement.scrollTop yourself.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

examples.label: Query current and maximum:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Webview.Scrollbar.getVerticalPosition"; "web" ) & " of " & MBS( "Webview.Scrollbar.getVerticalMaximum"; "web" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.Scrollbar.set
functions.description: Scrolls to the given coordinates. You can query the position using WebView.Scrollbar.getHorizontalPosition and WebView.Scrollbar.getVerticalPosition and then adjust if needed with this function to scroll to the right position in your web viewer. Alternative you can run JavaScript to call document.scrollingElement.scrollTo(x,y) yourself.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The new X position.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The new Y position.

examples.label: Scroll to top left:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.Scrollbar.set"; "web"; 0; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.SearchText
functions.signature: MBS( "WebView.SearchText"; WebViewerRef; Text { ; Backward; CaseSensitive; Wrap } )
functions.description: Searches text in a WebViewer. This is like the search field in Safari. Please note that this feature works fine in FileMaker 10, but has problems in FileMaker 11. Not supported for WebKit 2.x (FileMaker 16 Mac), but works in FileMaker 16, when using a Web Viewer created using WebView.Create .

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to search for.

parameters.name: Backward
parameters.description: A boolean value to determinate direction. Pass 0 for forward and 1 for backward. Default is 0. (This option has problems on Windows currently.)

parameters.name: CaseSensitive
parameters.description: Whether to search case sensitive. Pass 1 for case sensitive and 0 for case insensitive. Default is 0.

parameters.name: Wrap
parameters.description: A boolean value to decide whether to wrap around. If text is not found, we start search on the top again.

functions.result: Returns 1 if the text was found and 0 if not.

examples.label: Searches for text in WebViewer:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.SearchText"; "web"; "Hello")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.SelectAll
functions.description: Selects all text in web viewer. This is like choosing the menu command to select all, but without using the menu. As WebViewer functions often run asynchronously, you need to make a short script pause to let the WebViewer do the job.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

examples.label: Select all and copy:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Webview.SelectAll"; "web" ) & MBS( "Webview.Copy"; "web" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.SelectedText
functions.description: Queries text of current selection. If you put this in a button, the focus may leave WebViewer and selection is lost. In a menu it works better. See also WebView.GetPlainText , WebView.GetHTMLText and WebView.GetFormattedText .

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Formatted
parameters.description: Optional for Mac. If 1, tries to get text with formatting.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef

parameters.name: Value

parameters.name: Value

functions.name: WebView.SetCustomUserAgent
functions.description: Sets the custom user agent text. This user agent is reported to websites. By using a different user agent string you can tell a website that FileMaker is using Firefox, Chrome or Internet Explorer instead of Safari (WebKit). Add iOS with version 9.3. Implemented for Windows with Microsoft Edge/Chrome engine in MBS Plugin 11.5 when using FileMaker Pro 19.4 or newer. Please make sure the webviewer is already there. e.g. put in "about:blank" as URL in the layout editor. Then call WebView.SetCustomUserAgent in script to set th browser, before you load the final website.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: UserAgent
parameters.description: The new useragent.

examples.label: Set custom user agent to be Internet Explorer 7:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetCustomUserAgent"; "web"; "Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 7.0; Windows NT 6.0)" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.SetDrawsBackground
functions.description: Whether to the WebView draws background. If background drawing is turned off, the web viewer should allow transparent background. e.g. HTML site, where body has a transparent background color in CSS.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Flag
parameters.description: Pass 1 to draw background or 0 to allow transparency.

examples.label: Make transparent and then load web content with transparent body:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ("WebView.setDrawsBackground"; "web"; 0) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS (" WebView.LoadHTML "; "web"; "

Hello World 2

") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.setDrawsBackground"; "web"; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WebView.LoadHTML"; "web"; "<html><body style=\"background-color:transparent;\"><p style=\"background-color: white;\">Hello World 2</p></body></html>") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.SetEditable
functions.description: Sets whether the web viewer allows the user to edit its HTML document. You can change the receiver's document programmatically regardless of this setting. By default a WebView object is not editable. Normally, an HTML document is not editable unless the elements within the document are editable. This method provides a low-level way to make the contents of a WebView object editable without altering the document or DOM structure. Alternative you can run JavaScript to assign document.body.contentEditable yourself.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Editable
parameters.description: Whether to make the WebViewer editable or not. Pass 1 to make editable or 0 to make it not editable.

examples.label: Turn editing on for a WebViewer:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetEditable"; "web"; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.SetFormButtonValue
functions.signature: MBS( "WebView.SetFormButtonValue"; WebViewerRef; FormNameOrIndex; FieldNameOrIndex; Value { ; OnlyVisible } )
functions.description: Sets the value of a Button on the specified form To learn about forms and fields on a website, you can use the Form Utility application included with our plugin. Some websites use a lot of JavaScript, so you may need to send change event to control via WebView.RunJavaScript so the javascript is notified about the changes.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: FormNameOrIndex
parameters.description: Either the Name of the Form or the Index starting at 0

parameters.name: FieldNameOrIndex
parameters.description: You can either pass the field name of the field indix. 0 is the first item

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value for the button.

parameters.name: OnlyVisible
parameters.description: Whether to look for all fields or only the visible ones. Default is false.

functions.name: WebView.SetFormInputChecked
functions.signature: MBS( "WebView.SetFormInputChecked"; WebViewerRef; FormNameOrIndex; FieldNameOrIndex; Value { ; OnlyVisible } )
functions.description: Sets the value of the specified Check Box or Radiobutton. To learn about forms and fields on a website, you can use the Form Utility application included with our plugin. Some websites use a lot of JavaScript, so you may need to send change event to control via WebView.RunJavaScript so the javascript is notified about the changes.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: FormNameOrIndex
parameters.description: Either the Name of the Form or the Index starting at 0

parameters.name: FieldNameOrIndex
parameters.description: You can either pass the field name of the field indix. 0 is the first item

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to set the input to.

parameters.name: OnlyVisible
parameters.description: Whether to look for all fields or only the visible ones. Default is false.

examples.label: Load a website with login if needed:
examples.text_code:
# Load website
Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: "https://somedomain/search" ]
# Wait for it
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("
WebView.IsLoading
"; "web") ≠ 1 ]
End Loop
#
# Set login name
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.SetFormInputValue"; "web"; "login_form"; "__ac_name"; "xxx") ]
If [
MBS
("iserror") ]
# No form, so we are already logged in?
If [ Position (
$r
; "failed to find form" ; 1 ; 1 ) = 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Fehler" ;
$r
]
End If
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
# Set password
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.SetFormInputValue"; "web"; "login_form"; "__ac_password"; "yyy") ]
If [
MBS
("iserror") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Fehler" ;
$r
]
End If
# Wait
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
# Click login button
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.ClickInput
"; "web"; "login_form"; "submit") ]
If [
MBS
("iserror") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Fehler" ;
$r
]
End If
# or submit form
// Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WebView.FormSubmit
"; "web"; "login_form") ]
// If [
MBS
("iserror") ]
// Show Custom Dialog [ "Fehler" ;
$r
]
// End If
#
# Wait for page loading after login
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("
WebView.IsLoading
"; "web") ≠ 1 ]
End Loop
# Go to search page where we want to be after login
Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: "https://somedomain/search" ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load website</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="146" name="Set Web Viewer">
    <Action value="GoToURL"/>
    <ObjectName>
      <Calculation>"web"</Calculation>
    </ObjectName>
    <URL custom="False">
      <Calculation>"https://somedomain/search" </Calculation>
    </URL>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Wait for it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> MBS("webview.isloading"; "web") ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set login name</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetFormInputValue"; "web"; "login_form"; "__ac_name"; "xxx") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

examples.label: Login to a website:
examples.text_code:
Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: "https://some.domain.com/" ]
#
# wait for it to load
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
Loop [ Flush: Always ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebView.IsLoading
"; "web") ]
Exit Loop If [
$r
≠ 1 ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
End Loop
#
# no form name, so we use index 0
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.SetFormInputValue"; "web"; 0; "email"; "test@test.de") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebView.RunJavaScript
"; "web"; "var o = document.getElementById('email'); var evt = document.createEvent('Events'); evt.initEvent('change', true, true); o.dispatchEvent(evt); ") ]
# wait a bit
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
# and password
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("WebView.SetFormInputValue"; "web"; 0; "password"; "test@test.de") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebView.RunJavaScript
"; "web"; "var o = document.getElementById('password'); var evt = document.createEvent('Events'); evt.initEvent('change', true, true); o.dispatchEvent(evt); ") ]
# wait a bit
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,5 Duration (seconds): ,5 ]
# submit it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
WebView.FormSubmit
"; "web"; 0) ]
# continue with another IsLoading loop

functions.name: WebView.SetFormSelectValue
functions.signature: MBS( "WebView.SetFormSelectValue"; WebViewerRef; FormNameOrIndex; FieldNameOrIndex; Value { ; OnlyVisible } )
functions.description: Sets the value of a select element, such as a list or a combo box or a menu. Note: In HTML the Options of a Select tag can include a "value" attribute. If they do then this function sets the selection based on the value of the "value" attribute. To learn about forms and fields on a website, you can use the Form Utility application included with our plugin. Some websites use a lot of JavaScript, so you may need to send change event to control via WebView.RunJavaScript so the javascript is notified about the changes.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: FormNameOrIndex
parameters.description: Either the Name of the Form or the Index starting at 0

parameters.name: FieldNameOrIndex
parameters.description: You can either pass the field name of the field indix. 0 is the first item

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to set the input to.

parameters.name: OnlyVisible
parameters.description: Whether to look for all fields or only the visible ones. Default is false.

examples.label: Fill a select input item:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetFormSelectValue" ; "web"; "search"; "borough"; "BROOKLYN" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.SetFormTextAreaValue
functions.signature: MBS( "WebView.SetFormTextAreaValue"; WebViewerRef; FormNameOrIndex; FieldNameOrIndex; Value { ; OnlyVisible } )
functions.description: Sets the value of a Text Area Element. Please note that the this method does not work for other field types. To learn about forms and fields on a website, you can use the Form Utility application included with our plugin. Some websites use a lot of JavaScript, so you may need to send change event to control via WebView.RunJavaScript so the javascript is notified about the changes.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: FormNameOrIndex
parameters.description: Either the Name of the Form or the Index starting at 0

parameters.name: FieldNameOrIndex
parameters.description: You can either pass the field name of the field indix. 0 is the first item

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: the value you want to set

parameters.name: OnlyVisible
parameters.description: Whether to look for all fields or only the visible ones. Default is false.

functions.name: WebView.SetFormValue
functions.signature: MBS( "WebView.SetFormValue"; WebViewerRef; FormNameOrIndex; Field; NewValue { ; OnlyVisible } )
functions.description: Set the value of the specified Field in a WebViewer. To learn about forms and fields on a website, you can use the Form Utility application included with our plugin. Some websites use a lot of JavaScript, so you may need to send change event to control via WebView.RunJavaScript so the javascript is notified about the changes.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: FormNameOrIndex
parameters.description: Either the Name of the Form or the Index starting at 0

parameters.name: Field
parameters.description: the name of the field, case sensitive

parameters.name: NewValue
parameters.description: the value to set

parameters.name: OnlyVisible
parameters.description: Whether to look for all fields or only the visible ones. Default is false.

examples.label: Set A form value
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[

Field = "name";
NewValue = "Clark Kent";
Form = "myForm";
WebViewerName = "MyWebViewer";

WindowRef = MBS("Window.FindByTitle"; Get(WindowName));
WebViewerID = MBS("WebView.FindByName"; WindowRef; WebViewerName)

];


MBS("WebView.SetFormValue"; WebViewerID; Form; Field; NewValue)

)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetFormValue" ; "web"; "search"; "street_name"; "test2" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.SetFrameWithControl
functions.description: Repositions web view to given placeholder control. If you moved control in layout mode, this can move the web view back to the new position. Also useful to switch between different locations on the layout.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: ControlName
parameters.description: The name of the control on your FileMaker form. e.g. you can place a rectangle there to define the position.

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move view more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move view more to top or bottom.

functions.name: WebView.SetHeight
functions.description: Sets the height of the WebViewer. While this may be useful for e.g. to make a screenshot of a WebViewer with content rendered for that height, FileMaker Pro is not aware about the change and may reset it later at any time.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new height in points.

functions.description: Sets whether HiDPI handling is enabled. We can opt-in for your FileMaker installation to let web viewer use proper resolution handling for web content. You may just call this in your start script and it may take effect after the next restart of FileMaker.

parameters.name: Enable

functions.description: Sets the Internet Explorer version to use for web viewer. By default FileMaker uses IE7 for the web viewer, even if you have version 11 enabled.

functions.name: WebView.SetMediaVolume
functions.description: Sets the maximum volume of all sounds generated on this web view. Sets a master volume control for all media elements in the WebView. Valid values are 0..1.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new volume.

functions.name: WebView.SetMemoryUsageTargetLevel
functions.description: Sets MemoryUsageTargetLevel to indicate desired memory consumption level of WebView. Value is 0 for normal and 1 for low. Scripts will not be impacted and continue to run. This is useful for inactive apps that still want to run scripts and/or keep network connections alive and therefore could not call TrySuspend and Resume to reduce memory consumption. These apps can set memory usage target level to kMemoryUsageTargetLevelLow when the app becomes inactive, and set back to kMemoryUsageTargetLevelNormal when the app becomes active. It is not necessary to set CoreWebView2Controller's IsVisible property to false when setting the property. It is a best effort operation to change memory usage level, and the API will return before the operation completes. Setting the level to kMemoryUsageTargetLevelLow could potentially cause memory for some WebView browser processes to be swapped out to disk in some circumstances. It is a best effort to reduce memory usage as much as possible. If a script runs after its related memory has been swapped out, the memory will be swapped back in to ensure the script can still run, but performance might be impacted. Therefore, the app should set the level back to kMemoryUsageTargetLevelNormal when the app becomes active again. Setting memory usage target level back to normal will not happen automatically. An app should choose to use either the combination of TrySuspend and Resume or the combination of setting MemoryUsageTargetLevel to low and normal. It is not advisable to mix them. Trying to set MemoryUsageTargetLevel while suspended will be ignored. The TrySuspend and Resume methods will change the MemoryUsageTargetLevel. TrySuspend will automatically set MemoryUsageTargetLevel to low while Resume on suspended WebView will automatically set MemoryUsageTargetLevel to normal. Calling Resume when the WebView is not suspended would not change MemoryUsageTargetLevel.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 0 for normal and 1 for low.

functions.name: WebView.SetPageSizeMultiplier
functions.description: Sets the page size multiplier. Change the zoom factor of the page in views managed by this webView. This function is only for MacOS and iOS. Please use WebView.SetZoom for Windows.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: A fractional percentage value, 1.0 is 100%.

functions.result: Returns OK on success or error message on failure.

functions.name: WebView.SetPreferences
functions.description: Sets WebView preferences parameters. For WebKit 1.x you can pass empty WebViewerRef to access global preferences. Available keys for WebKit 1.x (FileMaker till version 15 and WebView.Create ): allowsAnimatedImageLooping, allowsAnimatedImages, cacheModel, cursiveFontFamily, defaultFixedFontSize, defaultFontSize, defaultTextEncodingName, fantasyFontFamily, fixedFontFamily, javaEnabled, javaScriptCanOpenWindowsAutomatically, javaScriptEnabled, loadsImagesAutomatically, minimumFontSize, minimumLogicalFontSize, plugInsEnabled, privateBrowsingEnabled, sansSerifFontFamily, serifFontFamily, shouldPrintBackgrounds, standardFontFamily, tabsToLinks, userStyleSheetEnabled or usesPageCache. Available keys for WebKit 2.x (FileMaker 16): shouldPrintBackgrounds, developerExtrasEnabled, logsPageMessagesToSystemConsoleEnabled, minimumFontSize, javaScriptEnabled, javaScriptCanOpenWindowsAutomatically, telephoneNumberDetectionIsEnabled, javaEnabled and plugInsEnabled. New in version 10.2: mediaDevicesEnabled and mediaStreamEnabled to allow camera/microphone/screen access. New in version 10.3: CrossOriginResourcePolicyEnabled and webSecurityEnabled to enable cross site scripting. Please check WebKit documentation for details. Implemented for Windows with Microsoft Edge/Chrome engine (WebView2) in MBS Plugin 11.5 when using FileMaker Pro 19.4 or newer. Possible keys: IsScriptEnabled, IsWebMessageEnabled, AreDefaultScriptDialogsEnabled, IsStatusBarEnabled, AreDevToolsEnabled, AreDefaultContextMenusEnabled, AreHostObjectsAllowed, IsZoomControlEnabled, IsBuiltInErrorPageEnabled, areBrowserAcceleratorKeysEnabled, IsPinchZoomEnabled, IsPasswordAutosaveEnabled and IsGeneralAutofillEnabled. MBS Plugin 11.5 adds fraudulentWebsiteWarningEnabled for macOS 10.15 and iOS 13.0 to set whether the warnings or fraudulent websites is enabled. The textInteractionEnabled setting in macOS 12.0 and iOS 15.0 allows to set whether text interactions are enabled. Added IsSwipeNavigationEnabled and HiddenPdfToolbarItems options in version 14.2 for Windows using WebView 2. If used with a touch display, the IsSwipeNavigationEnabled option can disable swiping since it is on by default. HiddenPdfToolbarItems allows you to disable toolbar items for when PDF documents show. Combine the following values to disable some toolbar items:

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: ParameterName
parameters.description: The name of the parameter.

parameters.name: ParameterValue
parameters.description: The value for the given parameter: A boolean value for allowsAnimatedImageLooping, allowsAnimatedImages, javaEnabled, javaScriptCanOpenWindowsAutomatically, javaScriptEnabled, loadsImagesAutomatically, plugInsEnabled, privateBrowsingEnabled, shouldPrintBackgrounds, tabsToLinks, userStyleSheetEnabled and usesPageCache. A number for cacheModel, defaultFixedFontSize, defaultFontSize, minimumFontSize or minimumLogicalFontSize. A string for cursiveFontFamily, defaultTextEncodingName, fantasyFontFamily, fixedFontFamily, sansSerifFontFamily, serifFontFamily or standardFontFamily.

functions.result: Returns OK or an error message.

functions.name: WebView.SetPrintParameter
functions.description: Sets print parameters for WebView.Print method. If showPrintPanel (Default 1) if set to 0, then no print window is shown. verticallyCentered and horizontallyCentered define if content is centered. If printer name is not valid, it will be ignored. Parameters for iOS: Duplex: None, LongEdge or ShortEdge, OutputType: General, Photo, Grayscale or PhotoGrayscale, JobName and Orientation: Portrait or Landscape. Parameters for Windows with Edge: Orientation: Portrait or Landscape. ShouldPrintBackgrounds, PaperWidth, PaperHeight, BottomMargin, TopMargin, LeftMargin, RightMargin, ShouldPrintHeaderAndFooter: 1 or 0, ShouldPrintSelectionOnly: 1 or 0, HeaderTitle and FooterUri Parameters for macOS with WebKit 2: HorizontallyCentered, VerticallyCentered, ShowPrintPanel, Orientation: Portrait or Landscape. PaperName, Printer, PaperWidth, PaperHeight, BottomMargin, TopMargin, LeftMargin, RightMargin, ShouldPrintBackgrounds and MinimumFontSize. Orientation can be portrait or landscape. Margins are in points (at 72 dpi). HeaderTitle: The default value is the title of the current document. If an empty string or null value is provided, no title is shown in the header. FooterURI: The default value is the current URI. If an empty string or null value is provided, no URI is shown in the footer. Use WebView.GetPrintParameter to query values.

parameters.name: ParameterName
parameters.description: The name of the parameter. For MacOS, can be horizontallyCentered, verticallyCentered, showPrintPanel, orientation, printer, bottomMargin, topMargin, leftMargin or rightMargin.

parameters.name: ParameterValue
parameters.description: The value for the given parameter: A boolean value for horizontallyCentered, verticallyCentered and showPrintPanel. "portrait" or "landscape" for orientation. The printer name for printer. A number for bottomMargin, topMargin, leftMargin or rightMargin.

functions.result: Returns OK or an error message.

functions.name: WebView.SetTitle
functions.description: Sets the title of the web page that is currently being displayed in the web Viewer. The same as document.write = "Something" Alternative you can run JavaScript to set document.title yourself.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to set

functions.name: WebView.SetUsePrivateBrowsing
functions.description: Sets private browsing desire. You call this before a WebViewer is created and make your wish. When FileMaker creates a WebViewer, we intercept and if you like to have private browsing, we switch to a non persistent data storage. Then we let FileMaker continue. You would do a script like this: Call MBS("WebView.SetUsePrivateBrowsing"; 1) Go to a layout with web viewer Load an URL Script pause for maybe 0.1 seconds to let FileMaker create web viewer. You can call WebView.SetUsePrivateBrowsing again pass 0. If you like to be all Web Viewers in FileMaker be private, just call WebView.SetUsePrivateBrowsing once on startup in your start script. The setting persists till you disable it or FileMaker quit. If you have two web viewer with both private browsing, you can login into the same website with two different accounts. Normally those would share the cookies and two accounts won't work.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable private browsing or 0 to disable.

examples.label: Try private browsing:
examples.text_code:
# enable private browsing for next Web Viewer created
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.SetUsePrivateBrowsing"; 1 ) ]
# Load a layout with Web Viewer
Go to Layout [ “WebViewerLayout” (Test) ; Animation: None ]
# Load a website
Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: "https://www.google.com/" ]
# And give FileMaker some time to create the web viewer!
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,3 ]
# Going back to default to regular data source.
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WebView.SetUsePrivateBrowsing"; 0 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> enable private browsing for next Web Viewer created</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetUsePrivateBrowsing"; 1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load a layout with Web Viewer</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="WebViewerLayout"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Load a website</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="146" name="Set Web Viewer">
    <Action value="GoToURL"/>
    <ObjectName>
      <Calculation>"web"</Calculation>
    </ObjectName>
    <URL custom="False">
      <Calculation>"https://www.google.com/" </Calculation>
    </URL>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> And give FileMaker some time to create the web viewer!</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,3 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Going back to default to regular data source.</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WebView.SetUsePrivateBrowsing"; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.SetVirtualHostNameToFolderMapping
functions.description: Add a virtual host mapping. Sets a mapping between a virtual host name and a folder path to make available to web sites via that host name.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef

parameters.name: FolderPath

parameters.name: accessKind

parameters.name: Value

functions.name: WebView.SetZoom
functions.description: Sets the optical scale of the webpage. This is the function for Windows. Please use WebView.SetPageSizeMultiplier , WebView.ZoomPageIn or WebView.ZoomPageOut for MacOS. See also WebView.SetInternetExplorerHiDPI function. Supported for Windows in FileMaker 19.4 to work with WebViewers with Edge engine.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Factor

functions.result: Returns OK on success or error.

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WebView.Version
functions.description: Queries WebKit version for web viewer. For FileMaker up to version 15, there is only WebKit 1.0 used. For FileMaker 16 or newer, FileMaker uses WebKit 2.0. Web viewers made with WebView.Create use WebKit 1.0 or 2.0. Returns 1 for older WebKit and 2 for newer WebKit.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

examples.label: Query version for web viewer named "web" on layout:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Webview.Version"; "web")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Webview.GetPreferredColorScheme
functions.description: Queries the preferred color scheme. The PreferredColorScheme property sets the overall color scheme of the WebView2s associated with this profile. This sets the color scheme for WebView2 UI like dialogs, prompts, and menus by setting the media feature prefers-color-scheme. The default value for this is "auto", which will follow whatever color scheme the OS is currently set to.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

functions.result: Returns light, auto or dark or error.

examples.label: Queries preferred color scheme:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Webview.GetPreferredColorScheme"; "web" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Webview.SetDefaultDownloadFolderPath
functions.description: Sets the default download folder path. The default value is the system default download folder path for the user. The default download folder path is persisted in the user data folder across sessions. The value should be an absolute path to a folder that the user and application can write to. Throws an exception if the value is invalid, and the default download path is not changed. Otherwise the path is changed immediately. If the directory does not yet exist, it is created at the time of the next download. If the host application does not have permission to create the directory, then the user is prompted to provide a new path through the Save As dialog. The user can override the default download folder path for a given download by choosing a different path in the Save As dialog.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The new download folder path.

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Webview.SetDefaultDownloadFolderPath"; "web"; "C:\Users\Christian\Downloads" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Webview.SetPreferredColorScheme
functions.description: Sets the preferred color scheme. The PreferredColorScheme property sets the overall color scheme of the WebView2s associated with this profile. This sets the color scheme for WebView2 UI like dialogs, prompts, and menus by setting the media feature prefers-color-scheme. The default value for this is "auto", which will follow whatever color scheme the OS is currently set to.

parameters.name: WebViewerRef
parameters.description: Either the Web Viewer Object Name or the Web Viewer ID as returned by " WebView.FindByName " function.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass auto, light or dark.
